X-rays are pictures of the spine. They may be taken to find injuries or diseases that affect the discs or joints in your spine. These problems may include spinal fractures, infections, dislocations, tumors, bone spurs, or disc disease. Spinal X-rays are also done to check the curve of your spine (scoliosis) or for spinal defects. are a form of radiation, like light or radio waves, that are focused into a beam, much like a flashlight beam. X-rays can pass through most objects, including the human body. Dense tissues in the body, such as bones, block (absorb) many of the X-rays and look white on an X-ray picture. Less dense tissues, such as muscles and organs, block fewer of the X-rays (more of the X-rays pass through) and look like shades of gray on an X-ray. X-rays that pass only through air look black on the picture. The spine is divided into four parts. So there are four common types of spinal X-rays: - Cervical spine X-ray. This X-ray test takes pictures of the 7 neck (cervical) bones. - Thoracic spine X-ray. This X-ray test takes pictures of the 12 chest (thoracic) bones. - Lumbosacral spine X-ray. This X-ray test takes pictures of the 5 bones of the lower back (lumbar vertebrae) and a view of the 5 fused bones at the bottom of the spine (sacrum). - Sacrum/coccyx X-ray. This X-ray test takes a detailed view of the 5 fused bones at the bottom of the spine (sacrum) and the 4 small bones of the tailbone (coccyx). The most common spinal X-rays are of the cervical vertebrae (C-spine films) and lumbosacral vertebrae (LS-spine films). We don't height play with the farm. One way to think about glacier is, they are rivers of ice. But sometimes glaciers can be much larger than rivers. They can be oceans of ice, like the glaciers that cover Antarctica and Greenland. What would an ocean of ice do to the land beneath it? What would the land look like when the glaciers melt away? Let's start by thinking about rocks in fast-moving streams. Stones in mountain streams are often round and smooth. How does that happen? As the water moves them, they bump into other stones. Their corners and edges get knocked off, and they get round as they roll down the stream. As sand rubs against them -and they rub against sand- the smallest edges and corners get knocked off too, and they become polished and smooth. That takes a lot of rolling, so it takes a lot of time. Now look at these pictures of mountains. The mountains on the bottom are all jagged and sharp. The mountains on the top are more round, more smooth. So which of these mountains are older? The ones that are round and smooth are older! Their edges have been worn off. What could wear a mountain down? And how long does it take? Water wears the mountain down. Water erodes it away. All those rocks moving down streams and rivers do not move back up the mountain. Sometimes the water is ice! A glacier! Glaciers are like bulldozers -actually, several lines of very slow bulldozers- and push huge amounts of rock downhill. Back to our first two questions: What would an ocean of ice do to the land beneath it? It would wear off the edges and corners of the land, making it rounder. What would the land look like when the glaciers melt away? It would look like the mountains in the top picture! Those worn-down mountains are in the Hudson Highlands in New York. There is good evidence that they were once covered completely by glaciers, sometimes a mile deep! These mountains were worn down by ice, lots and lots of ice, covering the whole landscape, pushing it down and wearing it away. The mountains on the bottom are from the Rocky Mountains, in Colorado. They are still growing! They are very young compared to the old, rounded mountains in New York. How long does it take to wear down a mountain? Well, the evidence shows that the rounded mountains in New York stopped growing 250 million years ago. (That's about 20 million years before the first dinosaurs.) So a good answer is, it takes a couple of hundred million years to wear a mountain down, to make a mountain smooth and round instead of jagged and sharp. The original EPOD can be found here. [Nina takes some of the play+dough peas out of the pot.] I don't know if it's maybe an otter. Hello young scholars! Today, let's learn about a fun concept called "Markov chains." Imagine you have a toy box with different colored balls inside. Each day, you randomly pick one ball out of the box, note its color, and then put it back. This process creates a sequence of colors - red, blue, green, and so on. With Markov chains, we study these kinds of sequences and their patterns! Let's talk about a special type of Markov chain called a "birth-death chain." It's like our toy box but with a twist; instead of putting the same number of balls back each time, after picking a red ball, we add more red balls into the box. Conversely, when choosing any other color, we take away some red balls. We call this a "red bias," which changes the chances of getting a red ball over time. Now imagine having a magical notebook where you write down your observations daily. You notice something interesting - no matter how many times you repeat this experiment, the long-term proportion of days spent picking red balls remains constant! That's because there exists a very cool thing called an "invariant measure" or "stationary distribution" describing those stable proportions. Our formula to calculate the stationary distribution for the red ball looks like this: \_n = (probability\_of\_adding\_red / probability\_of\_not\_adding\_red) ^ n * initial\_proportion Don't worry about understanding the math too deeply yet - just know that it shows us how likely we are to see a certain number of red balls in our toy box during our random picks in the future. Finally, here comes a riddle! Let's say you start observing on Monday with some known initial proportion of red balls in your toy box, denoted by \_0. Can you find a general expression for the chance of seeing 'n' red balls on Thursday? Yes, indeed! Using the recurrence relationship mentioned earlier, we derive the answer as follows: (n) = (probability\_of\_adding\_red / probability\_of\_not\_adding\_red) ^ n * \_0 And now, go ahead and explore the fascinating world of probabilities through toys and games! Remember, even though complex mathematical language may sound intimidating sometimes, concepts like Markov chains make sense once broken down into manageable pieces. Get GCSE Past Papers Online: A Comprehensive Tutorial ===================================================== This tutorial will guide you through the process of obtaining General Certificate of Secondary Education (GCSE) past papers online. These resources are essential for students preparing for GCSE examinations as they provide valuable practice material and insight into examination format and question styles. The following steps will help you find and download these documents with ease. Step 1: Identify the Exam Board ------------------------------- Before beginning your search, identify the relevant exam board that sets and marks the GCSE exams for your subject(s). Common UK-based boards include AQA, Edexcel, OCR, CCEA, and WJEC. Knowing the correct exam board is crucial since different boards may have varying questions, formats, and mark schemes. ### Key Tips * If unsure about which exam board applies to you, consult your teacher or refer to your school's curriculum documentation. * It might be necessary to obtain past papers from multiple sources if studying subjects under various exam boards. Step 2: Visit the Official Website ---------------------------------- Access the official website of the identified exam board using a web browser. Navigate to their 'Past Paper' section by exploring menus like 'Exams & Resources', 'Teachers', 'Students', or similar options. This section typically contains all available past papers categorized based on subjects and academic years. ### Guidelines * Be cautious when sourcing materials; only rely on reputable websites such as those operated by the actual exam boards themselves or trusted educational platforms. * Keep track of any required login details or membership subscriptions needed to access certain resources. Step 3: Select Relevant Subject and Academic Year ------------------------------------------------- Once inside the Past Paper section, select the appropriate subject area and academic year corresponding to your needs. For instance, if seeking Mathematics past papers for the 2018 series, choose this specific combination from the list provided. ### Key Points * Choosing the right academic year ensures consistency between study materials and current syllabi while providing updated examples of examination content. * Some boards offer combined or tiered (Foundation / Higher) past papers, so ensure selection matches your course requirements. Step 4: Download Mark Schemes and Specifications ---------------------------------------------- In addition to past papers, also consider downloading associated mark schemes and specifications. Mark schemes outline the methodology used to award grades for particular answers, whereas specifications detail what should be taught within a given subject over an academic period. Both resources aid understanding of assessment criteria and support effective revision strategies. ### Tips * Familiarize yourself with both mark scheme and specification terminologies to enhance comprehension during self-study sessions. * Utilize these tools alongside past paper exercises to gauge progress and pinpoint areas requiring improvement. Step 5: Save and Organize Downloaded Files ------------------------------------------ After selecting the preferred files, proceed with saving them onto your device. Create dedicated folders per subject and academic year to maintain organization throughout your revision journey. ### Suggestions * Adopt consistent naming conventions for easy identification later on. * Consider backing up important documents regularly to avoid data loss due to unforeseen circumstances. By diligently following these steps, you can successfully acquire GCSE past papers and related resources online. With regular practice and careful analysis of feedback, improved performance in upcoming examinations becomes increasingly attainable. Happy Studying! Find a User's Email Address on Facebook on a PC or Mac Facebook is one of the most popular social networking platforms with millions of active users worldwide. While many people use Facebook to connect with friends and family, others also utilize it for business purposes such as marketing, advertising, and communication. If you are looking to find someone's email address on Facebook, this tutorial will guide you through the process using a PC or Mac. Before we begin, it's important to note that privacy settings vary from user to user. Not all Facebook users make their contact information publicly available, so there may be instances where you cannot locate the email address you are searching for. Additionally, it's crucial to respect other users' privacy rights and only attempt to find an email address if you have permission to do so. Step 1: Log into Your Facebook Account To start, open your preferred web browser and navigate to facebook.com. Enter your login credentials (email address or phone number and password) and click on "Log In." This action grants you access to your account and allows you to search for other users. Step 2: Navigate to the User's Profile Page Use the search bar at the top of the page to type in the name of the person whose email address you wish to find. Once you see their profile appear in the dropdown menu, click on their name to visit their profile page. Make sure you have the correct profile before proceeding, especially if multiple users share the same name. Key tip: You can also search for a user by entering their full username directly into the URL after "www.facebook.com/" (e.g., "[www.facebook.com/username](http://www.facebook.com/username)"). Step 3: Check Basic Information On the user's profile page, look under their profile picture to view basic information about them. Sometimes, they might include their email address here, but more often than not, this section won't display any sensitive data due to strict privacy policies. However, it doesn't hurt to check! Step 4: Review About Section Click on the "About" tab below the user's cover photo. Scroll down until you reach the "Contact and Basic Info" section. Here, you may find various ways to get in touch with the user, including their email address. Keep in mind that users have complete control over what appears in this section; therefore, some profiles may not show any contact details. If no email address is visible, consider sending a friend request or messaging the user via Facebook Messenger to ask for their contact information. Remember always to maintain proper etiquette when communicating online. Step 5: Utilize Third-Party Tools There are several third-party tools designed explicitly to extract email addresses associated with public Facebook profiles. These services crawl through accessible data and gather relevant information based on specific parameters set by the user. Some popular options include Email Hunter, Snov.io, and Aeroleads. Please note that these services usually require payment plans or subscriptions, and results should always be verified since accuracy varies depending on the tool. In conclusion, finding a user's email address on Facebook requires patience and persistence. Always ensure that you comply with applicable privacy laws and regulations while attempting to retrieve personal contact information from another user's profile. Respect their decision regarding visibility and avoid engaging in actions deemed intrusive or malicious. Title: How to Crochet a Triangle **Things You'll Need:** * Yarn (preferably lightweight) * Crochet hook (size G or 4mm is recommended for this project) * Scissors * Tapestry needle **Step 1: Chain Three Stitches** To start your triangle, you need to create a foundation chain. Make a slip knot and chain three stitches. The slipknot counts as your first chain stitch. This initial row forms the base of your triangle. **Step 2: Single Crochet into Third Chain from Hook** Next, insert your hook into the third chain from the hook, yarn over, pull up a loop, then complete a single crochet stitch. It's important to note that we don't count the loop already on our hook when doing these steps - only after pulling up a new loop through both loops currently on our hook. **Step 3: Chain One** Chain one stitch. This extra chain counts as your turning chain and will help maintain the shape of the triangle. **Step 4: Double Crochet Two Times in Each Stitch Across** Now, double crochet twice into the next stitch. Repeat this process across the entire row until you reach the end. Remember to make two double crochets into each stitch including the last one before turning. At the end of this row, you should have four double crochet clusters. **Step 5: Turn Work and Chain Three** Turn your work so that you can begin the next row. Instead of chaining one like before, chain three this time. As mentioned earlier, this additional chain acts as both a space for the corner and your turning chain. **Step 6: Skip First Chain Space, Double Crochet Twice Into Next Chain Space** Skip over the first chain space created by your turn, then double crochet twice into the second chain space. **Step 7: Double Crochet Once in Each Remaining Chain Space** Continue down the row, doubling crocheting once into every remaining chain space. When you get to the end of the row, don't forget to double crochet twice into the final chain space. **Step 8: Repeat Rows 5-7 Until Desired Size** Keep repeating rows five through seven until your triangle reaches your desired size. For larger triangles, consider increasing the number of chains at the beginning of each row. **Key Tips & Guidelines:** * Always remember to tighten your slip knot but keep all other stitches loose enough for easy maneuvering with your hook. * Pay attention to where you put your stitches; misplaced stitches can cause irregularities in your pattern. * If you run out of yarn while making your triangle, simply join a new strand using a slip stitch. Be sure to leave a tail long enough for sewing later. * Practice consistent tension throughout your piece for uniformity and aesthetics. By following these comprehensive steps and adhering to the given advice, you'll be able to craft beautiful crocheted triangles suitable for various projects such as blankets, shawls, or even decorative wall hangings! Title: How to Teach Your Dog the "Sit" Command Introduction: Teaching your dog the "sit" command is one of the first steps in training them. It's simple, yet foundational for more complex tricks and behaviors. This guide will provide clear, detailed instructions on how to teach your dog to sit using positive reinforcement methods. Step 1: Choose the Right Time and Place Find a quiet area with minimal distractions where you can work with your dog. Make sure both of you are relaxed and free from stress. Training sessions should be short (5-10 minutes), frequent (2-3 times a day), and fun! Why this step matters: Starting off in a calm environment helps your dog focus on learning instead of being overwhelmed by external stimuli. Frequent but shorter sessions prevent boredom or mental exhaustion. Step 2: Get Your Dog's Attention Hold a treat close to your dog's nose without letting him eat it. Say "sit" in a firm, friendly tone while slowly moving the treat upwards and backwards over his head. Key Tip: The movement of the treat encourages your dog to tilt its head back, naturally causing it to sit as it tries to follow the treat. Step 3: Reward Desired Behavior As soon as your dog's bottom touches the ground, give him the treat and lots of praise. Repeat this process several times during each session until he starts sitting promptly when you say "sit." Guideline: Timing is crucial here. Giving the reward immediately after the correct behavior reinforces that action. Step 4: Add Verbal Cue Beforehand Once your dog responds well to the hand signal, add the verbal cue before giving the hand signal. Over time, gradually phase out the hand signal so that only the verbal command remains. Explanation: Associating the word 'sit' with the actual action strengthens the command's effectiveness even without visual cues. Step 5: Practice Regularly Regular practice solidifies the trick into memory. Try incorporating the 'sit' command into daily routines like mealtime, playtime, or whenever they want something. Reason: Consistent practice maintains what your dog has learned and generalizes the behavior to different contexts. Step 6: Introduce Distractions Gradually Slowly introduce mild distractions (like another person entering the room) while practicing. If your dog loses concentration, return to a less distracting environment until he masters the trick there again. Purpose: Training under various conditions prepares your dog for real-world situations where obedience is necessary despite distractions. Conclusion: Patience and consistency are key when teaching your dog to sit. Remember, every dog learns at its own pace. Never punish your pet if progress seems slow; instead, reevaluate your approach and make adjustments accordingly. With these steps, you'll have a well-trained companion who understands and follows the 'sit' command happily. It may seem strange that ants have uses, but there are some. Some people use ants for food, medicine and rituals. Some species of ants are used for pest control (they eat pests that destroy food for humans). They can damage crops and enter buildings, though. Title: How to Perform Intercourse: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: Intercourse is an intimate act between two consenting adults that can lead to physical and emotional bonding. It's important to approach this activity with respect, care, and open communication. This guide will provide you with clear steps and helpful tips on how to perform intercourse safely and enjoyably. Remember, the most crucial aspect of any sexual encounter is mutual consent, so always ensure both parties are comfortable before proceeding. Step 1: Establish Consent Key Tip: Open Communication Before engaging in any sexual activity, make sure both partners have given their explicit consent. Discuss boundaries, preferences, and safe words if necessary. Clear communication ensures a positive experience for all involved. Step 2: Create a Comfortable Environment Key Guideline: Set the Mood Create a relaxing atmosphere by dimming lights, playing soft music, or using scented candles. Make sure the room temperature is suitable for both partners. A comfortable environment allows for better focus on intimacy and pleasure. Step 3: Foreplay Key Tip: Take Your Time Foreplay helps prepare the body for intercourse by increasing blood flow and promoting relaxation. Engage in activities such as kissing, touching, and oral sex to build anticipation and enhance overall satisfaction. Step 4: Lubricate Key Guideline: Use Lube Generously Lubricant reduces friction during intercourse, making it more pleasurable and less painful. Apply lubricant to the genital area or choose condoms pre-lubricated with spermicide for added protection against STIs and pregnancy. Step 5: Choose a Position Key Tip: Experiment Safely There are various positions for intercourse; some popular ones include missionary, doggy style, and cowgirl. Each position offers different levels of intimacy, stimulation, and control. Always prioritize comfort and safety when trying new positions. Step 6: Penetration Key Guideline: Go Slowly During penetration, take things slowly to allow both partners to adjust. Pay attention to your partner's reactions and communicate openly about what feels good and what doesn't. Step 7: Maintain Rhythm Key Tip: Synchronize Breathing Find a rhythm that works for both partners. Synchronizing breathing can help maintain this harmony and increase pleasure. Don't be afraid to experiment with pace and intensity. Step 8: Climax Key Guideline: Embrace the Moment Allow yourself to fully experience the sensations leading up to climax. If one partner reaches orgasm before the other, continue gently moving until both partners are satisfied. Step 9: Aftercare Key Tip: Be Supportive After intercourse, spend time cuddling, talking, or simply enjoying each other's company. Offer reassurance, compliments, and support to strengthen your connection. Conclusion: Performing intercourse requires patience, understanding, and effective communication. By following these steps and keeping your partner's needs in mind, you can create a satisfying and memorable experience for both of you. Always remember to practice safe sex and seek medical advice regarding contraception and regular STI testing. But there was no time it started raining. I think I know what all this stuff here is. Title: How to Give Effective Feedback to Walmart Introduction: Providing feedback is an essential part of improving any business, including retail giant Walmart. Whether you're an employee, customer, or stakeholder, your input can help shape policies, products, and services. This tutorial will guide you through providing constructive and helpful feedback to Walmart. Step 1: Identify Your Role Determine whether you are giving feedback as a customer, employee, or another type of stakeholder (e.g., investor, community member). Each role may require different channels and methods for sharing feedback. * Key Tip: Being clear about your relationship with Walmart allows you to tailor your feedback accordingly and increases its relevance and impact. Step 2: Determine What Type of Feedback You Want to Provide Consider if your feedback pertains to a specific experience, product, service, policy, or general observation. Narrowing down the focus makes your feedback more actionable and easier to address. * Guideline: Avoid combining multiple unrelated issues into one piece of feedback; instead, submit separate comments for each topic. Step 3: Choose the Appropriate Channel Select the appropriate method for submitting your feedback based on your role and topic. Here are some common options: * Customers: Use the Walmart website's Contact Us page, email customer service, write a review on their social media pages, fill out a survey at the bottom of receipts, or talk directly to store management during visits. * Employees: Share concerns with direct supervisors first, then escalate to HR or upper-level managers if necessary. Utilize open communication platforms provided by the company, such as suggestion boxes or regular meetings. * Stakeholders: Reach out via official contact information found on Walmart's Investor Relations or Corporate Governance websites, attend shareholder meetings, or engage with local representatives who have connections with the corporation. Step 4: Prepare Your Message Organize your thoughts before communicating them. Consider using this structure: 1. Begin with a positive statement acknowledging something that works well or express gratitude for addressing past feedback. * Example: "I appreciate the wide selection of organic produce available at my local Walmart." 2. Clearly state your concern or idea without being accusatory or overly emotional. Focus on facts rather than personal opinions. * Example: "However, I noticed that the freshness of these items varies greatly from day today." 3. Offer potential solutions or suggestions if applicable. Demonstrating possible ways to resolve the issue showcases your investment in improvement. * Example: "Perhaps implementing daily quality checks could improve consistency." Step 5: Communicate Politely but Assertively Deliver your message respectfully while still emphasizing its importance. Maintain professionalism throughout your interaction. * Key Tip: Be patient and understanding if faced with questions or delays. Remember, change takes time, especially within large organizations like Walmart. Step 6: Follow Up As Needed If you don't receive a response after an acceptable period-usually two weeks for customers and employees-follow up with another polite reminder. Persistence shows dedication to resolving the issue. * Guideline: Keep records of all correspondence related to your feedback for future reference. Conclusion: By following these steps, you can provide effective feedback that contributes positively to Walmart's operations, products, and services. It's crucial to remember that every voice matters, so take advantage of opportunities to communicate openly and honestly with the company. Maybe later we can go out again and pat them. Do you have any questions about XXX? Becoming a good film director requires a combination of creativity, technical knowledge, and strong leadership skills. Here's an in-depth guide to help you get started: Step 1: Develop Your Visual Storytelling Skills Good film directors are masters at visual storytelling - they convey narratives through imagery rather than just words. To develop this skill, watch films from various genres and eras, paying close attention to aspects like camera angles, lighting, editing, and color palette. Analyze how these elements contribute to the overall story and emotional impact. Additionally, practice photography or drawing as these activities can enhance your ability to see and create compelling compositions. Step 2: Understand the Craft of Filmmaking Familiarize yourself with all aspects of film production including screenwriting, cinematography, sound design, acting, and post-production. This understanding will enable you to communicate effectively with your crew members and make informed decisions during shooting. Consider attending workshops, short courses, or even earning a degree in film studies. Step 3: Read Extensively About Directing Techniques There are numerous books written by experienced directors who share their insights into directing techniques, challenges faced on set, and advice for aspiring directors. Some recommended titles include "Directing Actors" by Judith Weston, "In the Blink of an Eye" by Walter Murch, and "The Conversations: Walter Mitch & Michel Ciment" by Michael Ondaatje. Step 4: Gain Experience Working On Sets Start small - volunteer as a runner, grip, or PA (Production Assistant) on student projects, low-budget indies, commercial sets, etc. Every role offers valuable lessons about teamwork, problem-solving, and respecting deadlines. Over time, aim to assist more senior roles such as assistant director or script supervisor. Step 5: Create Short Films Write, shoot, and edit your own short films to hone your directing chops. These don't need big budgets; many successful directors began making shorts using basic equipment. Use these opportunities to experiment with different styles, test out new ideas, and build a portfolio showcasing your talent. On car and truck tires, they are marked with 3 numbers and might look like: 225/60R16. The first number is the width in millimeters of the tire at the widest point when it is mounted and inflated. The second number is the sidewall (side of the tire) height as a ratio or percentage of the width. The last number is the wheel diameter in inches. Title: How to Create a Milkmaid Braid A milkmaid braid is a classic and elegant hairstyle that can be worn for various occasions, from casual outings to formal events. This hairstyle involves two braided sections wrapped around the head like a crown. With patience and practice, you'll master this beautiful look. Follow these steps to create your own milkmaid braid. ### Sectioning Your Hair 1. Brush your hair thoroughly: Before starting any braid, make sure to remove all tangles and knots using a detangling brush or a wide-tooth comb. Starting with smooth hair will help you avoid breakage and ensure an even distribution of tension throughout the braid. 2. Divide your hair into two equal sections: Using a rat-tail comb or your fingers, part your hair down the middle creating two equally sized halves - one on either side of your head. Secure both sections with hair ties or clips to keep them separate during the braiding process. Key Tip: Make sure the parting line runs parallel to your eyebrows for symmetry and balance. ### Braiding Each Section 3. Begin French braiding the first section: Remove the clip from one half of your hair. Grab a 3-inch (7.6 cm) piece of hair near your forehead. Divide this portion into three equal strands. Cross the right strand over the middle strand, then cross the left strand over the new middle strand. As you continue crossing strands, incorporate additional hair into the braid by grabbing small amounts from the outer edge of your head. Keep adding more hair until you reach the nape of your neck. 4. Finish off as a regular braid: Once all of your hair has been incorporated into the French braid at the nape of your neck, finish braiding the remaining length of your hair regularly without incorporating any extra hair. Secure the end of the braid with a clear elastic band. 5. Repeat on the other side: Now move onto the second half of your hair. Following the same technique used on the first section, complete a French braid ending with a traditional three-strand braid. Secure the end with another clear elastic band. Key Guideline: Maintain consistent tension while braiding to prevent loose areas or gaps in your finished milkmaid braid. ### Wrapping the Braids Around Your Head No, no these are his toys, right, he's allowed to play with these. The name of Saint Nicholas is now remembered in many countries as Santa Claus. See he's got his silky right there? yeah, but I ain't going to do it. So you're four years old is that right? He knew this must be the one. So Jack said "this one is brilliant!". Daddy smiled and said: "You're right Jack! That's the one I was going to pick too!". Jack was so proud of himself. To find the GDP of a country, one adds up all consumer spending (C), all investment (I), all government spending minus taxes (G), and the value of exports minus imports ("X - M"). This is shown by the equation:formula_1 This measure is often used to find out how healthy a country is; a country with a high value of GDP can be called a large economy. The United States has the largest GDP in the world. Germany has the largest in Europe, Nigeria in Africa and China in Asia. This revival has been driven largely through the creation of Irish-language schools. The Irish language is spoken by some nationalists (whether Catholic or Protestant) people. What are arrow cards and what do you do with them? Most teachers are familiar with place value charts and base ten blocks for helping to build, reinforce, and extend place value concepts. Teachers in the United Kingdom also use arrow cards to support place value skills and concepts, and this useful tool is beginning to catch on in the United States as well. Let's take a look at how to make, use, and explore with arrow cards. Arrow cards are a set of place value cards with an "arrow" or point on the right side. Students can organize the cards horizontally or vertically to represent numbers in expanded notation. They can overlap cards and line up the arrows to form multi-digit numbers. Images courtesy of Wendy Petti. When arrow cards are color-coded by place, they are easier to organize, and the color helps reinforce the concept of place. A yellow "3" looks and feels different from an orange "30" and a red "300." Students in primary grades can work with arrow cards up to the hundreds or thousands, while students in upper elementary grades might benefit from using arrow cards up to 6, 7, 8, or even 9 digits. Arrow cards form a useful transition between base ten blocks and written numerals. Base ten blocks are very concrete. They look exactly like their value. Using base ten blocks, 300 is represented with 3 hundred squares; each hundred square is the size of 10 tens or 100 ones. An arrow card representing 300 is larger than arrow cards representing tens or ones, but not in proportion to their value; it is simultaneously concrete and abstract. It is a physical and numerical representation emphasizing both the place and the value of the 3. There are several online sources for arrow cards up to the thousands. Print and photocopy the arrow card templates onto card stock, which is stiffer and more durable than regular copy paper. You might use colored card stock, but another option is to print the arrow cards onto white card stock and have students lightly color the cards with an agreed-on color scheme, using colored pencils or crayons. Another coloring option would be to trace around the numerals with colored markers. The printable accompanying this article is a multi-page spreadsheet holding arrow card templates up to the hundred millions. The collection includes three place value charts -- to the thousands, the hundred thousands, and the hundred millions -- sized for use with the arrow cards. Cutting out the arrow cards is efficient because they touch each other. Print only the pages you need, of course. Keep it simple at first, even in the upper grades. Number Downloads from Brenden Is Teaching Includes both colored and black-and-white arrow cards. Arrow Cards from Tim Whiteford Large and easy to cut; to thousands. When students first begin working with arrow cards, use them hand-in-hand with base ten blocks so students can see and feel the value of each number. At the outset, you might use a large set of arrow cards for whole class demonstrations, while pairs of students assemble numbers using smaller sets. You also might like to magnetize your demonstration set to use on a metal chalkboard in expanded notation form; you can purchase rolls of magnetic tape at craft stores. When you layer the cards, you'll need to hold them. Students should sort the cards into groups according to place -- ones, tens, hundreds, and thousands -- and each group should be a different color. Work through a series of "show me" activities, in which students at first hold up single arrow cards and then hold up numbers theyve built. "Show me 8. Show me 40...60.... How many tens are in 60? Show me 700...." Build a few two-digit numbers. "Show me 11, 12... 46, 47...." Remind students that when they build 46, the 40 is still there. Its 40 + 6. Forty is 4 tens; 46 is 4 tens plus 6. Set up the same number with base ten blocks. After introductory work, students should be able to follow instructions with minimal teacher modeling as needed. Move to "show me" activities in which student pairs build two related numbers: "Show me 35 and 53." Both numbers use a 3 and a 5. Discuss the difference in the value of 5 as it is used in each number. Break the numbers apart to look at their components. Extend the idea. "Show me all the 3-digit numbers you can make with a 3, 5, and 7 in any place." (How many sets of arrow cards will a pair of students need for this activity, in order to display all of the arrangements?) "Show me all the 2-digit and 3-digit numbers you can make using 3, 5, or 7 in any place." After building each number, students should keep a written record so they can reuse the same cards. After students show a few 3-digit and 4-digit numbers, ask them to show a number with zeroes. "Show me 104. Show me 6,018. Show the year you were born." In the number 104, the 0 means an absence of tens. We don't need a "zero tens" card to show this. We can show 104 with 100 + 4; when we put them together, we will see a zero in the tens place, from the 100 card. Add and Subtract "Show me 10 more than 13...20 more than 13." If students arrange the tens cards in sequence, they can pull away each ten to reveal the next, while the 3 remains stable in the ones place. Model the same numbers with base ten blocks. "Show me 10 less than 97...20 less...." "Show me 100 more than...100 less than...1000 more than...1000 less than...." You can transition to the language, "Build a number that is...." "Build a number that is 40 more than 39....Build a number that is 300 less than 415...." For these activities, using a place value chart and two sets of cards, student pairs can arrange the original number and the new number. Roam the room to check for understanding; students cannot hold up the place value chart and two numbers. Discuss with students, "How can we build a number that is 60 more than 278?" If they count by tens, what happens after 288, 298....? Students should be able to see they will need to trade to the 300 arrow card in order to continue. Building on that idea, what is 70 + 60? (Is it easier to think in terms of 70 + 30 + 30?) What is 270 + 60? What is 278 + 60? Students should be able to use arrow cards and mental math rather than paper and pencil calculations. Using this same strategy, but taking things step-by-step, what is 278 + 64? How can we build a number that is 50 less than 432? What is 30 less than 430? How can we adjust the number from there? What is 20 less than 400? What is 20 less than 402? When students build numbers beyond the thousands, it might help to paperclip the arrow cards together to keep them aligned. The place value chart will be very helpful in reinforcing the value of each place. In working with larger numbers, more of the questions are likely to do with multiples of 10: What is 100 times greater than 320? What number, when multiplied by 1,000, becomes 960,007,000? What is the value of the 6? It can be a challenge even to say those large numbers; give students plenty of practice. Students need to be able to understand and apply place value skills and concepts to respond to most of the "show me" and "build a number" tasks. Stretch students' thinking skills even more with questions having more than one response. "Build a number whose digits add up to 21." "Build a 3-digit number in which the tens digit is two less than the hundreds digit and the ones digit is two less than the tens digit. What is the highest number you can build? The lowest number?" "What numbers can you make smaller than 100 that have 7 in the tens place?" "Can you find several ways to build 1,436 as the sum of smaller numbers?" (For example: 1,000 + 200 + 230 + 6...) "I'm a number between 600 and 900 with one zero. What number could I be?" "Build a 5-digit number that is a multiple of 10." "Build a number that is 100 times larger (or 1,000 times larger) than the last number you built.",br> "What is the largest 6-digit number you can build that has 3 zeroes, but is not a multiple of 1,000? What is the smallest?" Finally, get your students' creative juices flowing with this one! "Can you create a new arrow card activity and share it with the class?" Some of the arrow card activities are adapted from an unpublished manuscript and private communication with Tim Whiteford, PhD, who has collected some outstanding elementary math resources and links at: Math Education Some of the place value extension questions were inspired by the place value questions in Good Questions for Math Teaching: Why Ask Them and What to Ask (K 6) by Peter Sullivan and Pat Lilburn, pages 33-36. A small house is often called a cottage. Apart from it, its also called small space. In England, where this word comes from, it is used to mean a house that has one main storey, with a second, lower storey of bedrooms which fit under the roof upstairs. Cottages are usually found in villages or in the countryside. They are nearly always built from material that can be found nearby. In many places the word cottage is used to mean a small old-fashioned house. In the United States the word cottage is often used to mean a small holiday home. Papal can pull the little red wagon. The first garden gnome was created in Switzerland. It was later introduced to the United Kingdom in 1847 by Sir Charles Isham, when he brought 21 terracotta figures back from a trip to Germany and placed them around the gardens of his home at Lamport Hall in Northamptonshire. Only one of the original batch of gnomes survives. His name is Lampy. He is on display at Lamport Hall, and is insured for one million pounds. Would you rather fight a horse-sized duck or 100 duck-sized horses? I asked my kids and they had a very interesting discussion. For me, the answer is the horse-sized duck. Now I will tell why physics helps me make this decision (even though I don't actually know the answer yet). How Big Is a Horse-Sized Duck? That right there is a tough question. What kind of horse is it? What kind of duck? Are the duck and the horse the same height or the same mass? Is it the same height with a duck in the normal position or with the neck stretched out? No one knows. Let me start with the types of animals. When I think of duck, I think of this: This is a male and female mallard duck. According to Wikipedia, the mallard is 50 to 65 cm long with a mass of 0.72 to 1.58 kg. I'm not sure what "long" means. Is that the duck stretched out or in a normal position? Wikipedia also lists the bill as having a length of about 4.5 cm. With that and the above picture, I get a height of about 27 cm. What about a horse? Yes, Wikipedia has a page on horses. From this, I am going say the "average" horse has a height of 1.57 meters (height at the shoulders) and a mass of 500 kg. Same Height as a horse What if the duck and the horse are the same "size"? I am going to say that the duck and the horse would have the tops at the same vertical level as shown in the image above. From this image, I also estimated the width of the huge duck. Looking at the duck image, I measure a leg radius of about 0.005 meters. What is the compression pressure in these legs for a normal duck? It would be weight of the duck divided by the total cross-sectional area. Let me just use one leg because at some point (like for a walking duck), the duck would have to put all the weight on just one leg. That might seem high, but it really isn't. Wikipedia lists bones as having a compressive strength about 1,000 times higher than this (human bones - but still). So, ducks can walk. If we ramp this up to our horse-sized duck, what happens? The mass increases and so does the radius of the leg. The horse-sized duck is 6.85 times larger than a duck. The leg would also be 6.85 times larger. This should make walking pretty tough. I think this duck would just sit there quacking - but really loud quacks. I could just toss some rocks at it until I was declared the winner. Title: Create a Fart Noise Using Your Hands Creating a fart noise using your hands is a fun and harmless prank that you can play on friends or family members. This simple trick, also known as "making a trumpet" or "playing the hand flute," requires only your two hands and some practice. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to do it. ### Step 1: Find a Quiet Space Find a quiet space where you won't be disturbed while practicing this skill. Practicing in a noisy environment may make it difficult for you to hear the subtle sounds you are trying to create. ### Step 2: Position Your Dominant Hand Place your dominant hand with your palm facing upwards. Curl your fingers slightly towards your palm, leaving a small gap between your thumb and index finger - large enough for air to pass through when you blow. This forms the basic shape of the makeshift instrument. Key Tip: The size of the gap affects the pitch of the sound. A smaller gap creates a higher pitched sound, while a larger gap produces a lower pitched one. Adjust accordingly based on the type of fart noise you want to mimic. ### Step 3: Place Your Non-Dominant Hand Take your non-dominant hand and place it flat against the back of your dominant hand, covering all fingers but the thumb and index finger area (the 'bell' of your hand flute). Make sure there is good contact between both hands so that the air doesn't escape elsewhere. Guideline: Keep your nails short to avoid discomfort during practice and performance. ### Step 4: Pucker Your Lips Pucker your lips as if you were going to kiss someone. This position directs the stream of air directly into the opening between your thumbs. ### Step 5: Blow Air While Modifying the Shape Start blowing gently through pursed lips into the opening. At the same time, experiment by changing the shape of the hole with your free hand. You might need to adjust the curvature of your fingers or widen/narrow the gap slightly to get the right sound. Key Tips: - Start with gentle blows; too much force could result in an unpleasant loud noise instead of a controlled fart sound. - Listen carefully to the sound produced after each attempt. It will help you understand what modifications are needed. ### Step 6: Practice Regularly Like any other skill, creating a convincing fart noise takes regular practice. Spend time every day perfecting your technique until you can consistently produce the desired sound. Remember, although making fart noises can be entertaining, ensure you use this newfound talent responsibly and respectfully. During the Permian, these continents were connected as Gondwana. The most common ways are: Title: How to Evolve Dragonair in Pok mon Games Dragonair is a beloved Pok mon that many trainers want to evolve into its final form, Dragonite. This guide will provide you with an in-depth explanation of how to evolve your Dragonair in various Pok mon games. **Step 1: Catch a Dragonair** To start, you need to catch a Dragonair. You can obtain one by either trading with another player or by evolving Dratini, which requires 25 candies. Dratinis are available in the wild in various games such as Pok mon Go, Let's Go Pikachu/Eevee, Sword/Shield, etc. Once you have a Dragonair, you're ready to move onto the next step. **Step 2: Gain Experience Points (XP)** Before your Dragonair can evolve, it needs to reach level 55. To do this, gain experience points through battles, catching other Pok mon, using Rare Candies, or participating in trainer battles. The more XP your Dragonair earns, the closer it gets to evolution. Remember, your goal is to help your Dragonair learn new moves and grow stronger while earning enough XP to hit level 55. **Key Tips:** * Grinding experience in areas with high encounter rates can speed up the process. * Use EXP Share items to distribute experience points among all party members, even those who didn't participate in battle. * Participate in Trainer Battles, Gym Leaders, or Elite Four battles for significant XP boosts. **Step 3: Acquire an Evolution Stone (Optional)** In some Pok mon games like FireRed/LeafGreen or HeartGold/SoulSilver, certain special stones can be used to force evolution. However, these methods aren't necessary for Dragonair because it naturally evolves at level 55 without any stone requirements. Still, if you wish to explore alternative evolution methods, look into acquiring a Moon Stone or Thunder Stone depending on the game version. **Step 4: Reach Level 55** Keep training your Dragonair until it reaches level 55. At this point, it will automatically evolve into Dragonite during battle or outside of battle when switching out. Note that evolution may occur mid-battle, potentially changing the tide of combat. Be prepared for strategic adjustments once your Dragonair turns into Dragonite! **Guidelines:** * Make sure your team is well balanced before attempting to evolve Dragonair. Its evolved form, Dragonite, has powerful stats but could leave gaps in your strategy if unprepared. * Consider teaching Dragonair crucial HMs or TM moves prior to evolution since Dragonite cannot learn them natively. **Step 5: Enjoy Your Newly Evolved Dragonite** Congratulations! After following these steps, you now possess a mighty Dragonite. Utilize its impressive abilities and devastating attacks wisely as you continue your journey. With proper care and training, your Dragonite can become a true asset to your team. Remember, patience and dedication pay off when raising strong Pok mon companions. Happy adventuring! Being an "It girl" isn't about following trends or being perfect; instead, it's about embracing your individuality, expressing yourself creatively, and having a positive impact on those around you. This guide will provide actionable steps and valuable insights to help teenage girls become self-assured, fashionable, and influential individuals within their communities. Step 1: Develop Confidence * Explanation: A strong sense of self-confidence is crucial when striving to be an "It girl." When you believe in yourself, others are more likely to trust and respect you. Moreover, confidence enables you to take risks and make bold decisions that set you apart from the crowd. * Key Tips & Guidelines: + Practice daily affirmations to reinforce positive thoughts and beliefs about yourself. + Set achievable goals and celebrate accomplishments to build self-esteem. + Surround yourself with supportive friends and family members who uplift you. + Engage in activities that challenge you and push you out of your comfort zone. + Learn to accept compliments graciously without downplaying your achievements. Step 2: Discover Your Personal Style * Explanation: An essential aspect of becoming an "It girl" involves cultivating a unique and authentic personal style. By experimenting with different clothing items, accessories, and beauty products, you can create a look that reflects your personality and interests. * Key Tips & Guidelines: + Research various fashion styles to find inspiration, but avoid copying other people's looks exactly. Creating New Switch Control Gestures on an iPhone Switch Control is a powerful accessibility feature available on iPhones that allows users with motor limitations to interact with their device using a variety of switches. This tutorial will guide you through creating new switch control gestures on your iPhone, providing in-depth explanations for each step and helpful tips along the way. Step 1: Open Settings App Locate and tap on the gray gear icon labeled "Settings" on your home screen or app library. The settings app is where you can customize various aspects of your iPhone's functionality. Step 2: Navigate to Accessibility In the settings menu, scroll down until you find the "Accessibility" option (it should be under the "General" section) and select it. Here, you can modify features designed to assist individuals with disabilities. Step 3: Select Switch Control Under the "Physical & Motor" category within Accessibility, choose "Switch Control". This feature lets you use external switches connected via Bluetooth or built-in ones like Home button, volume buttons, etc., to navigate and control your iPhone. Step 4: Tap 'Switches' On the Switch Control page, click on "Switches". Switches are used to perform actions when activated; they could be physical buttons or virtual ones created from existing controls. Step 5: Add New Switch Tap on "+Add New Switch" at the bottom right corner. You'll now have the opportunity to configure a new gesture for controlling your phone. Step 6: Choose Type of Switch You'll need to decide between two types of switches: "External" or "Internal". An External switch refers to hardware devices paired via Bluetooth. For this tutorial, we assume you want to create a software-based internal switch, so tap on "Internal." Step 7: Set Activation Method Choose how you wish to activate this switch by selecting one of the options presented - these include tapping the screen, moving the cursor, tilting the device, etc. We recommend choosing something comfortable and intuitive based on your abilities. Step 8: Configure Additional Options Depending on the activation method chosen, additional configuration options may appear. Fill out any necessary fields here. Remember, personal comfort and ease of use should dictate your choices. Step 9: Assign Function to Switch After setting up the switch itself, it's time to assign a function to it. Go back to the main Switch Control page and hit "Scanner Menu," then "Customize Commands." From there, you can set what action(s) triggering the switch will perform. Key Tips and Guidelines: 1. Test Each Step Thoroughly: Make sure everything works as expected before proceeding to the next step. It saves lots of trouble later! 2. Consider Comfort: Opt for methods requiring less effort whenever possible. After all, convenience is part of the point of customizing these controls. 3. Experiment Safely: Feel free to experiment with different configurations until you find the perfect fit. Just ensure safety while doing so, especially if adjustments affect movement or orientation sensors. 4. Document Your Changes: Keep track of changes made, including which switch corresponds to which command. It makes troubleshooting easier and ensures consistency across multiple sessions. By following these steps and keeping our key tips in mind, you've successfully created a brand new switch control gesture tailored specifically to your needs! Enjoy exploring enhanced functionality and increased independence during everyday tasks on your iPhone. I'm going to get up on his soldiers [shoulders]. Did you want' 'o tell a story? Title: How to Change the Time and Date on an Android Tablet or Phone Android devices are highly customizable and allow users to change various settings according to their preferences. Among these adjustable features is the time and date setting. This feature can be particularly useful when you travel across different time zones or if your device's clock is incorrect due to software issues. Here's our comprehensive guide on changing the time and date on your Android tablet or phone. **Step 1: Accessing Settings ** * Navigate to your home screen by pressing the circular button at the bottom center of your device (usually represented with a house icon). Alternatively, press the back button repeatedly until you reach the home screen. * Locate and tap on the "Settings" app. It may vary in appearance but generally includes a gear icon. Accessing settings gives you control over numerous aspects of your Android device including network connections, display options, sound settings, etc. From here, we will navigate specifically to the 'Date & Time' section. **Step 2: Entering the System Section ** * Scroll down until you find the 'System' category (could also be labeled as 'About Phone'/'About Device'). Tap on it. * Under this menu, look for 'Additional settings' or 'Advanced'. Not all devices have these sub-menus; if found, click into them. These steps lead us closer to the time and date settings. By accessing the system settings, we ensure that any changes made affect the entire operating system rather than just individual applications. **Step 3: Changing Date & Time ** * Within the system menu, locate and select 'Date & Time'. Some models might place this under 'General management', 'Language & input', or even directly within the main system settings page. * Once inside the 'Date & Time' settings, toggle off 'Automatic date & time' and/or 'Automatic time zone'. The exact wording could differ slightly based on manufacturer and version of Android OS. Disabling these two features allows manual manipulation of both the date and time. Disabling automatic settings provides flexibility, enabling users to set dates and times according to specific needs - whether traveling internationally or experiencing technical glitches. **Step 4: Setting New Values ** * After disabling auto mode, tap on 'Set date' and 'Set time' respectively. Input new values using the provided interface. Remember to consider daylight saving where applicable. * If needed, manually choose a timezone from the list provided after tapping on 'Select time zone'. Type the name of the city in the search bar for quicker location selection. Setting accurate timestamps ensures proper synchronization between apps, email servers, social media platforms, and other connected services. Correctly configured timezones prevent scheduling conflicts during communication and collaboration across borders. **Key Tips and Guidelines:** - Always double-check your entries before confirming. Erroneous data can cause confusion and potential complications. - When returning to your normal routine post-travel, remember to reactivate the automatic date & time option. Leaving it disabled permanently may result in unexpected behavior from certain applications. - Consider syncing your calendar events with Google Calendar or another cloud-based service. Many smartphones automatically update their clocks upon connecting to WiFi networks, minimizing the need for manual intervention. By following this detailed tutorial, you should now feel confident in changing the time and date settings on your Android tablet or phone! "How you must be afraid you'll chuck me!" Title: How to Group Apps on Android Android devices offer many customization options, including grouping apps into folders on your home screen. This can help you organize your apps, making them easier to find and access. Here's a detailed guide on how to do this: **Step 1: Long Press on an App Icon** Start by finding the app you want to move. Once you've found it, lightly press and hold down on the icon. Don't tap too hard or quickly - if done correctly, the app icons will start jiggling, indicating they're ready to be moved. Key Tip: Make sure your phone isn't set to 'Google Assistant' mode (where long pressing summons Google Assistant instead of enabling moving). You can check this under Settings > System > Gestures. **Step 2: Drag the App Icon** While still holding down on the icon, drag it slightly away from its original position. The other apps should also continue jiggling, signifying that they too can be moved. Guideline: Be careful not to lift your finger off the screen while doing this as it may cause the app icon to return to its original place. **Step 3: Create a New Folder** Drag the selected app over another app you wish to include in the same folder. When you release your finger, both apps will automatically form a new folder with the first app's label. Key Information: If there are existing folders, you can add more apps directly to these without creating a new one by simply dropping the dragged app onto the target folder. **Step 4: Edit the Folder Name** Once the folder is created, a text box will appear above the folder allowing you to rename it. Type in a name that describes what kind of apps are stored inside. For example, if it contains social media apps, name it "Social Media". Important Note: Editing the folder name immediately after creation is crucial because once you navigate away from the page or hit the back button, changing the folder's name becomes less straightforward. **Step 5: Add More Apps to the Folder** To add additional apps to the newly formed folder, open the folder and repeat steps 1-3 within the confines of the folder. To remove an app from a folder, simply drag it out of the folder to any empty space on your home screen. Additional Tips: - Organize related apps together like productivity tools, entertainment, travel, etc., which makes navigating through numerous apps simpler. - Keep decluttering your home screen regularly by removing unused apps from groups and uninstalling those no longer needed. - Remember where you placed frequently used folded apps so you don't waste time searching for them every time. By following these simple yet effective steps, you can maintain an organized Android device that suits your needs perfectly! He went to Eugene, Oregon, where he graduated from North Eugene High School and attended the University of Oregon. Sycamore is fast growing and regarded by some as a 'weed'. It is not a native tree but was introduced from central and south east Europe. It is tolerant of 'sea spray' and may be planted near the coast. Its heavy leaf fall can mean that the ground flora underneath the tree is limited. Simple but large leaves. The leaves are arranged in opposite pairs around the twigs/stem. 5 distinct lobes to the leaves, 5 veins radiate from the leaf base into the lobes. There is a somewhat ragged edge to the leaf, lots of 'rounded' teeth. On the lower surface of the leaf, there are some hairs. Sometimes the leaves are covered with small, red 'blobs' / projections - these are galls caused by a mite (a small spider-like creature).The female mite lays eggs in these structures. Buds, Bark and Stem Flowers and Fruits The flowers appear at the same time as the leaves. They are yellow/green and there are separate clusters of male and female flowers; they are carried on drooping 'tails' or tassels. When fertilised, they form winged seeds. These occur in pairs and form a helicopter structure that helps dispersal. The seed and its wing like structure is sometimes referred to as a 'key'; botanically speaking - it is a double samara. Title: Decide If Your Child Should Play MovieStarPlanet Introduction: MovieStarPlanet is an online social game targeted towards children aged 8-15 years old where they can create their own movie star avatar, make movies, play games, chat with friends, and earn Fame and StarCoins (the virtual currency). As a parent or guardian, deciding whether your child should play this game requires careful consideration. This tutorial will guide you through important steps to help you make an informed decision. Step 1: Research the Game's Content Before allowing your child to play MovieStarPlanet, research its content by visiting the official website and reading reviews from other parents and players. Pay attention to the following aspects: * Graphics and design * Age appropriateness of themes and activities * Interaction between users (chat features) Key tip: Make sure that the game aligns with what you consider suitable for your child based on age, maturity level, and personal values. Step 2: Understand the Gameplay Mechanisms Learn about various elements within the game, such as: * Creating and customizing an avatar * Making movies using pre-made backgrounds, props, and animations * Engaging in mini-games * Participating in chats and user interactions * Earning and spending StarCoins and Diamonds Guideline: Familiarize yourself with these mechanics so you understand both the fun aspect and potential risks associated with them. Step 3: Assess Safety Measures Examine the safety measures implemented by MovieStarPlanet, including: * Moderation tools and policies against cyberbullying * Privacy settings for controlling visibility and communication options * Reporting mechanisms for suspicious behavior or explicit content Key tip: Encourage open conversations with your child regarding safe internet practices while playing the game. Step 4: Evaluate Educational Value Determine if MovieStarPlanet offers any educational value for your child, such as: * Developing creativity through making movies and designing outfits * Enhancing problem-solving skills via mini-games * Learning basic economic concepts like earning, saving, and spending virtual currencies Step 5: Consider Time Management Consider setting time limits for daily gaming sessions to prevent excessive screen time and promote balance in your child's life. Establish clear expectations around when and how long they are allowed to play MovieStarPlanet. Key tip: Encourage participation in offline activities alongside online engagement to foster well-rounded development. Step 6: Monitor User Experience If you decide to let your child play MovieStarPlanet, monitor their experience regularly by checking in on their progress and discussing any concerns or challenges they encounter. Some questions to ask include: * Are they enjoying the game? * Have they faced any issues or negative experiences? * How do they handle successes and failures within the game environment? Guideline: Open dialogue allows you to address potential problems early and ensure a positive gaming experience for your child. Step 7: Stay Updated on Changes Keep up-to-date with changes made to MovieStarPlanet by periodically reviewing updates, newsletters, or community announcements. Adjust your supervision and guidance accordingly based on new features or policy modifications. Conclusion: By carefully examining the content, gameplay, safety measures, educational value, time management, user experience, and staying updated on changes, you can determine if MovieStarPlanet is appropriate for your child. Remember that every child has unique needs and interests; therefore, tailor your approach based on your family's specific circumstances. When energy moves from one form to another, the amount of energy always remains the same. It cannot be made or destroyed. This rule is called the "conservation law of energy". For example, when you throw a ball, the energy is transferred from your hand to the ball as you release it. But the energy that was in your hand, and now the energy that is in the ball, is the same number. For a long time, people thought that the conservation of energy was all there was to talk about. What was their average age? 2) Each little pig wanted to build a house. Pig #1 wanted to build a house of straw. Straw costs $4 a bundle. He needs 9 bundles. How much will he spend? 3) The 2nd little pig wanted to build a house of sticks. Each bundle of sticks weighs 5 pounds. Pig#2 needs 12 bundles. How much will they weigh? 4) Pig #3 wanted to build a house of bricks. Each side of his 4-sided house needs 100 bricks. How many bricks will he need? 5) How many different ways could the pigs arrange their houses? 6) Pig #1 worked on his house 3 hours a day for 10 days. "Let's run for home!" The pigs ran and ran. They ran 10 miles in 5 minutes. How many miles did they run each minute? 23) When they got home, Pig #1 heard a knock at his door. "Little Pig, Little Pig let me in!" (Everyone) "Not by the hair of my chinny chin chin!" Now the wolf was angry. He huffed and puffed and blew the house down! Title: How to Be a Popular School Girl Introduction: Being popular in school isn't just about having the most friends or being part of the 'cool' group. It's about being respected, liked, and influential in a positive way. This guide will help you become a beloved figure at your school while staying true to yourself. Remember, popularity should never come at the expense of kindness, integrity, or academic success. Step 1: Develop Good Study Habits * Explanation: Academic achievement is crucial for gaining respect from both teachers and peers. By showing dedication towards studies, you demonstrate responsibility and commitment. * Tips: Create a study schedule, take notes during class, form study groups, complete homework promptly, review material regularly, ask questions when needed, utilize resources like tutors if necessary. Color is the aspect of things that is caused by differing qualities of light being reflected or emitted by them. To see color, you have to have light. When light shines on an object some colors bounce off the object and others are absorbed by it. Our eyes only see the colors that are bounced off or reflected. The sun's rays contain all the colors of the rainbow mixed together. This mixture is known as white light. When white light strikes a white crayon or marker barrel, it appears white to us because it absorbs no color and reflects all color equally. A black crayon or marker cap absorbs all colors equally and reflects none, so it looks black to us. While artists consider black a color, scientists do not because black is the absence of all color. All light rays contain color. Light is made of electromagnetic waves. These waves spread out from any light source, such as the sun. Light waves travel at tremendous speed (186,000 miles or 300,000 kilometers per second). Different colors have different wavelengths, which is the distance between corresponding parts of two of the waves. The longest wavelength of light that humans can see is red. The shortest is violet. Ultraviolet has an even shorter wavelength, but humans cannot see it. Some birds and bees can see ultraviolet light. Infrared has a longer wavelength than red light, and humans can not see this light but can feel the heat infrared generates. Hello young mathematicians! Today, we're going to learn about something called "exponent rules." You may have seen expressions like "2 squared" or "3 cubed," but do you know what they really mean? Let's find out! An exponent is a small number written above and to the right of a base number. The base tells us which number to raise to a power, while the exponent tells us how many times to multiply that number by itself. For example, "2 squared" means "2 multiplied by itself twice," which equals 4. We write it as 2^2. Similarly, "3 cubed" means "3 multiplied by itself three times," which equals 27. We write it as 3^3. Now, let's talk about some important exponent rules. These will make working with exponents much easier and more fun! 1. Product rule: When you multiply numbers with the same base, add their exponents. For example, 2^3 \* 2^2 = 2^(3+2) = 2^5. This simplifies to 32 \* 4 = 128, so 2^3 \* 2^2 does equal 2^5! 2. Quotient rule: When you divide numbers with the same base, subtract their exponents. For example, 2^4 / 2^2 = 2^(4-2) = 2^2. This simplifies to 16 / 4 = 4, so 2^4 / 2^2 does equal 2^2! 3. Power rule: When you raise a number with an exponent to another exponent, multiply the exponents. For example, (2^3)^2 = 2^(3\*2) = 2^6. This simplifies to 8^2 = 64, so (2^3)^2 does equal 2^6! These rules apply not just to whole numbers, but also to variables with exponents, like x^m or y^n. Just remember to follow these rules carefully, and you'll become an expert at using exponents before you know it! Want to practice your newfound skills? Try solving these problems: 1. Simplify (3^2) \* (3^4). 2. Simplify 5^6 / 5^2. 3. Simplify ((x^2)^3)^2. 4. Simplify (ab^2) \* (a^3b^4). 5. Simplify 2y^3 / y. Remember, take it one step at a time, following each rule carefully. Happy calculating! A family is a group of people who, in most cases, live together. They share their money and food and are supposed to take care of one another. Its members are either genetically related (like brother and sister) or legally bound to each other, for example by marriage. In many cultures, the members of a family have the same or a similar surname. D' you want' me stand slippery bathtub? oh, you like those, are they good? So I can have some room here. He has four presents to buy, and he always waits ya know. And the other goes on the right. In order to follow rules and understand limits, children need to develop self-control. Self-control is the ability to cope with strong feelings and stop one's self from doing something that is not allowed. Developing self-control begins at birth and continues throughout childhood. Young children learn self-control through interactions with peers and guidance from parents and other loving adults. Two-Year-Olds and Self-Control Older toddlers are making great strides in developing self-control but still have a ways to go to learn to manage their impulses in appropriate ways. While they are beginning to understand what is and is not acceptable, they still do not have the full ability to stop themselves from doing something that's not allowed. Here is an example: A 2-1/2-year-old wants the toy that his friend is playing with. He grabs it. His friend grabs it back. He hits his friend. They both begin to cry. His mother calms him and then helps him return the toy to his friend. She explains that hitting is not okay and helps him ask for a turn with the toy. At this stage, setting and enforcing rules consistently becomes very important. Age-appropriate rules let children know what to expect, which makes them feel safe, secure and in control-key ingredients for their healthy social-emotional development. Experiencing consistent responses to behaviors also helps children make good decisions. What you can do when an incident happens: Stay calm. The calmer you are, the calmer the children will be. Children need you to be their rock when they are "losing it." Try a distraction. Ignore the kicking and screaming and do something unpredictable that the child isn't expecting. Ask her to join you in a game, help you get snack ready, or take a look out the window to see the pretty bird. Or just go to her and give her a big hug. Suggest taking a break. Some children actually calm down much more quickly when they can be by themselves in a safe place. This is not punishment. It helps children learn to soothe themselves. This safe, quiet space, the "cozy corner" if you will, might have some pillows, stuffed animals, books and small, safe toys. When the child pulls himself together, tell him what a good job he did calming himself down. Reconnect. Use a warm, caring tone of voice. Give the child a hug. Tell her what a good job she did dealing with her angry feelings. Discuss what happened and problem-solve. You were really angry and you kicked your friend. It's okay to feel angry, but kicking is not okay. Kicking hurts. Brainstorm different ways to handle the situation next time: Let's give Justin some of his own cups to play with so he won't mess up yours. Everyday ways to nurture self-control: - Suggest ways to manage strong emotions. When a child is really angry, suggest that he jump up and down, rip paper, cuddle up in a cozy area for alone time, paint an angry picture or another strategy that you feel is appropriate. Teach children that there are many healthy, non-hurtful ways to express their feelings. - Look for ways to practice self-control.Turn-taking games such as rolling a ball back and forth require children to wait and control their impulses. Take turns hitting a soft foam ball off a tee. Play "sharing music" where each of you chooses an instrument to play and set an egg-timer for 1 minute. When the timer goes off, switch instruments and set the timer again. - Give choices to head off misbehavior. For example, when a child is having trouble sharing a ball, you might say, You have a decision to make. You can choose to take turns with the ball or put the ball away. Keep your language firm while positive, simple and at the child's level. Help him understand he has choices, and that every choice has a consequence. - Offer alternatives. Help toddlers meet their goals in acceptable ways. It's not okay to throw blocks. Someone might get hurt. Let's throw these foam balls into a basket instead. Offering an alternative is important because toddlers need help identifying more appropriate activities. - Be consistent. Consistency with rules is key to helping children learn to make good choices. If every time a child throws a toy it gets taken away, she quickly learns not to throw toys. But when the rules keep changing, it is hard for young children to understand which rules are "for real." - Give children a visual to help them cope with waiting. If you'd like children to brush their teeth for 2 minutes each day after lunch, use an egg timer so they can watch the countdown. Need 10 minutes to call a parent? Set a timer so that children have a concrete way of understanding how long they have to wait.This helps them feel more in control and therefore able to cope better. - Look for patterns in children's behavior. Sometimes you can identify patterns that signal a child is likely to "lose it." Anticipating these breakdowns can help you prevent them. For example, if you notice that a child has a hard time coping with transitions, such as going from lunch to nap, you can give him a warning 5 minutes before. It can also be very helpful to establish a ritual that helps him make the change, such as reading a book or starting a drawing together that you finish when he wakes up. - Don't do something, just stand there. If a child is having a tantrum, take 15 seconds to stop and think before reacting. Ask yourself: What is my goal here? What do I want the child to learn from this experience? Taking the time to think it through can help you respond in a calmer more thoughtful and more effective way. oh, what does he have in his hand? " Put me in the zoo.
